# Execution Plan — Auditable Claim Ledger (2026-07-13)

Derived from `docs/RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md` frozen at commit `f049041`. Claim IDs use `<chunk>:L<line>` in that frozen copy.

Frozen source SHA-256: `29081cfff772e8fb7faf01a3395f651b41c5165e7573c2c83af181b4e8400111`. Durable source bundle: [`docs/audits/execution_plan_2026-07-13/source_artifacts.json`](audits/execution_plan_2026-07-13/source_artifacts.json). Deterministic builder/verifier: [`scripts/build_execution_plan_audit.py`](../scripts/build_execution_plan_audit.py).

## Provenance and scope caveat

The checklist claim source is commit-pinned and hash-verified. The recovered registry and critique prose was produced on 2026-07-13 against a concurrently changing local worktree, not a commit-pinned code snapshot. Treat code-state statements as audit findings whose **Required receipt** must be rerun before a checkbox or production behavior changes.

The P1L, P2X, and P3A critique JSON files were generated only by three surviving, pre-authored scripts. Their exact source, source hashes, output hashes, and output rows are preserved in the durable artifact bundle. No missing critique was newly written during salvage.

The original concept-flow writer failed before producing an artifact. This document therefore uses an honest mechanical `when_s` index and does not infer replacement family-to-claim flow prose. `docs/PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md` remains the companion S0-S14 sequencing rationale.

How to audit a ledger row: open its frozen line, run or inspect its Required receipt, and confirm its Disposition.

## Concept & Terminology Registry

This section is a mechanical render of the 10 recovered terminology families plus the leftover-term sweep. No names or mappings were added during salvage.

They agree on plain multi-word names but DIVERGE on hyphen/underscore/unicode names: 'Box2D-Lite' -> graph 'entity:box2dlite' vs lexicon fallback 'entity:box2d-lite'; and the lexicon fallback skips the curated entity_aliases.json merge entirely. (a2) superseded_at ALREADY exists with a different meaning: job-lease supersession (backend/services/ingestion/job_leases.py:435, job_control.py:60,113) and chunk superseded_by (contracts.py:115) — three lifecycles will share one word once fact_assertions.superseded_at lands; keep them namespaced by collection. | fully_enriched | FULLY_ENRICHED_STAGES = {"complete", "fully_enriched"} @ backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py:112 (stage-set that counts as fully enriched (legacy alias 'complete' folded in)); status = "fully_enriched" (corpus-level status derivation) @ backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py:672-673 (corpus readiness status value when fully_enriched >= doc_total); fully_enriched = min(fully_enriched, lexicon_ready / summary_tree_index_ready / document_profile_index_ready) @ backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py:368-454 (fully_enriched is min-gated by lexicon + Qdrant index projection readiness); final_stage = "fully_enriched" if verified_complete @ backend/services/ingestion/worker.py:5002-5005 (worker promotes a document's ingest_stage only after mongo+qdrant+summaries+neo4j+verification); QUERYABLE_INGEST_STAGES (contains fully_enriched) @ backend/services/ingestion_service.py:39-47 (queryable is a WEAKER set than fully_enriched (pending graph/summary variants included)); _PHASE_TO_STAGE {"complete": "fully_enriched"} and state["fully_enriched"] bool @ backend/services/ingestion/batches.py:83-84,424,562-581 (batch item state exposes a third, boolean form of the same word) | Mongo documents.ingest_stage = 'fully_enriched' (per-document stage enum); Mongo corpus_readiness.status = 'fully_enriched' (per-corpus rollup, collection corpus_readiness, schema corpus_readiness.v2); Mongo ingest_batch_items / ingest_batches document state.fully_enriched (boolean) | SAME | fully_enriched | complete (legacy stage alias, readiness.py:112); verified_complete (worker-local flag) | Failure mode (a): one string, three scopes — document ingest_stage value, corpus readiness status value, and a batch-state boolean. Nearest seams: (1) librarian_cards presence per doc (card_builder.py), (2) the S2/T-HOOK-3 bibliographic backfill fields once they land on documents, (3) facet-precision leftovers from P0.5 decontamination. Must be a NEW leg of corpus_readiness, gated separately from fully_enriched per S6. | grep -rniE 'metadata[_-]quality\|operational_readiness' backend/ --include='*.py' returns nothing; card_builder.py has zero readiness references (no publication gate either). |

### Collisions and duplicate-implementation risks

- **aliases & vocabulary — alias:** The core concept is clean: an alias is extraction-derived identity evidence for one lexicon entry. But the bare word 'alias' is heavily overloaded elsewhere — see the 'aliases' row. **Evidence:** corpus_lexicon.py:1-18 docstring: 'Aliases are identity evidence.' Alias fields flow Mongo->Qdrant via _lexicon_payload (qdrant_writer.py:1432).
- **aliases & vocabulary — alias registry:** FAILURE MODE (b) risk: three curated alias data stores ALREADY exist (CONCEPT_ALIASES in query_semantics.py:527, entity_aliases.json neo4j_writer.py:43, canonical_families.json neo4j_writer.py:46) plus the machine-built corpus_lexicon. If S5 builds the registry as a new store instead of absorbing these, it becomes the 4th/5th parallel alias system — exactly what checklist line 817 forbids ('Keep one corpus-lexicon system; do not create a parallel concept store'). **Evidence:** PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:84 (S5: 'corpus_lexicon/vocabulary join -> versioned alias registry -> gated query-expansion consumption; fold P0.5 curated-aliases-out-of-Python into the same registry'). No code symbol named registry exists (grep alias_registry = 0 hits). Nearest existing seams: corpus_lexicon materialize_entries (corpus_lexicon.py:1593) for storage+epoch invalidation, and the versioned-data pattern of shelf_policy_data.py:3 ('DATA, not logic: owner-curated, versioned as a unit').
- **aliases & vocabulary — aliases:** FAILURE MODE (a): one word, five different concepts in five keyspaces. Only meaning 1 is normalize_identity-keyed and retrieval-live; meaning 3 is captured-but-unread; meanings 2 and 5 are separate curated stores. Any plan sentence saying just 'aliases' is ambiguous. **Evidence:** grep -rn aliases across backend/services hits corpus_lexicon.py, query_semantics.py:527, summary_semantics.py:100, facets/normalizer.py:438, neo4j_writer.py:43 — five independent producers, no shared type.
- **aliases & vocabulary — canonical_key:** FAILURE MODE (a) adjacent: 'canonical' also appears in the GRAPH keyspace — canonical_name/canonicalize_entity_name (neo4j_writer.py:848, NFKD + curated alias map) which is NOT the normalize_identity keyspace. canonical_key always means the lexicon keyspace; graph 'canonical name' is a different normalization. **Evidence:** shelf_policy_data.py:13 and chat_orchestrator.py:3568-3572 both document the shared keyspace contract explicitly.
- **aliases & vocabulary — corpus_lexicon:** Name drift, not concept drift: the Qdrant mirror lives in the 'schemas' collection family (corpus_{cid8}_schemas, settings.QDRANT_SCHEMAS) under kind='entity_lexicon' — three names (lexicon/schemas/entity_lexicon) for one store depending on layer. **Evidence:** corpus_lexicon.py:1-19 docstring defines both collections; qdrant_writer.py:1490 'Mirror Mongo lexicon entries into the isolated schemas collection.'
- **aliases & vocabulary — curated aliases:** FAILURE MODE (a)+(b): the phrase 'curated aliases' names the retrieval-side CONCEPT_ALIASES in checklist lines 63/312/465 but the graph-side canonical_families docstring (neo4j_writer.py:945) uses the identical phrase for a different mechanism in a different keyspace (canonicalize_entity_name, NFKD, alias-map). The S5 registry migration must state which of the three curated stores it absorbs. **Evidence:** RAPTOR checklist:63 'Curated aliases belong in versioned data, not Python conditionals'; :312 'Move remaining curated aliases/rules into versioned lexicon/policy data'; :465 'Move remaining curated content aliases from Python into a versioned lexicon dataset'.
- **aliases & vocabulary — entity_id:** FAILURE MODE (b) CONFIRMED IN CODE: two id builders exist. They agree on plain multi-word names but DIVERGE on hyphen/underscore/unicode names: 'Box2D-Lite' -> graph 'entity:box2dlite' vs lexicon fallback 'entity:box2d-lite'; and the lexicon fallback skips the curated entity_aliases.json merge entirely. Any lexicon entity_ids minted by the :567 fallback can fail the vocabulary.py:851 Neo4j join silently — same class of bug as the underscore-slug incident fixed in b40da18. **Evidence:** neo4j_writer.py:1112-1127 vs corpus_lexicon.py:567-569; normalizer divergence shown at neo4j_writer.py:812-817 vs corpus_lexicon.py:192-198.
- **aliases & vocabulary — lexicon entry:** P2.1 (checklist:890-901) plans SPLITTING one entry into multiple representation points (name_alias/definition/utility) each carrying canonical_key+lexicon_id — today one entry = one point with concatenated glosses. Same concept, planned finer granularity; not a duplicate store as long as lexicon_id back-pointers stay mandatory (checklist:846,896). **Evidence:** corpus_lexicon.py:1780-1785 (lexicon_id), :2059 unique (corpus_id,lexicon_id) index; checklist:846 'Keep Mongo corpus_lexicon authoritative and make every Qdrant concept [point] removable'.
- **aliases & vocabulary — normalize_identity:** DISTINCT from the graph normalizer normalize_entity_name (neo4j_writer.py:812: NFKD, keeps underscores because \w matches _, deletes hyphens WITHOUT inserting a space). Example divergence: 'Box2D-Lite' -> normalize_identity 'box2d lite' vs normalize_entity_name 'box2dlite'. Two legitimate keyspaces, but code that mixes them silently breaks joins (see entity_id row). **Evidence:** corpus_lexicon.py:192-198 vs neo4j_writer.py:812-817 — different unicode form, different hyphen/underscore treatment.
- **aliases & vocabulary — query expansion:** FAILURE MODE (b) watch: S5 plans 'gated query-expansion consumption' of the new alias registry. The gate machinery ALREADY exists (grounded threshold + rejected_expansions + original-lane protection + lexicon-ID rejection, checklist:597). S5 should feed registry aliases through this existing gate, not build a second expansion path. **Evidence:** checklist:54 'Query expansion cannot replace or semantically drift from the original'; query_plan.py:5 'keeps the original user query as the mandatory recall lane'.
- **aliases & vocabulary — strong match:** Plan-vs-code staleness: PLAN_CRITIQUE:106 says the positional 6-match cap fix 'is still open (L)', but the fix IS in code (vocabulary.py:1705 'Audit Delta 2' comment, grounded_planner.py:375). Checklist:171-172 phrases the same thing as 'obligation-aware selection'. One concept, three names, and the critique doc is behind the code. **Evidence:** vocabulary.py:1705-1709 documents the 0.909-at-position-14 drop this fixes — the exact incident PLAN_CRITIQUE:106 cites as unfixed.
- **aliases & vocabulary — vocabulary:** FAILURE MODE (a): 'vocabulary' means the query-time resolver in P1.7/P1.2 checklist items but the materialized store in the corpus_lexicon docstring and in 'concept vocabulary' (checklist:31). S5's 'corpus_lexicon/vocabulary join' only parses if vocabulary = resolver-facing projection of the same lexicon; it is not a second store. **Evidence:** vocabulary.py:1-7 ('Corpus-grounded vocabulary resolution... reads only the corpus's Qdrant entity_lexicon points'); corpus_lexicon.py:1; checklist P1.7:699-724.
- **evaluation & gates — baseline:** FAILURE MODE (a): one word, three artifact species. In P1.1/S8 ('A/B on the held-out 56 vs the 2026-07-13 baseline', PLAN_CRITIQUE:87) 'baseline' = EVAL_*.json quality scores. In the Implementation Log ('Baseline capture (pre-edit requirement)', CHECKLIST:1272) 'baseline' = the census BASELINE_*.json + LATENCY_*.json. P1.2's acceptance 'improves materially over baseline' (CHECKLIST:611) means the EVAL numbers. A reader who fetches the wrong species will compare counts against recall rates. **Evidence:** All three writers target docs/baselines/ with distinct filename prefixes; capture_raptor_baseline.py:14, probe_tier_latency.py:22, run_heldout_eval.py:289-291.
- **evaluation & gates — before/after:** FAILURE MODE (a): CHECKLIST:1246 'Candidate counts before/after fusion' uses the identical words for per-run pipeline-stage counts inside one query — unrelated to change gating. FAILURE MODE (b): 'before/after evals' (shelf_reserve), 'A/B gate + golden battery' (waterfall), and P1.1's acceptance gate (CHECKLIST:591-592, unchecked) all describe the same missing comparison harness under three names — real risk each feature grows its own one-off A/B script. **Evidence:** P1.1's only acceptance box — 'No ranking... change ships without a before/after result on this suite' (CHECKLIST:591-592) — is open; grep finds no script that loads two EVAL_*.json files and diffs them.
- **evaluation & gates — contamination firewall:** FAILURE MODE (a): 'contamination' means two unrelated things. Here = eval-set leakage into production representations. In P0.5 it = facet metadata pollution ('corpus-lens facet contamination', CHECKLIST:118; backend/scripts/p0_5_facet_decontamination.py). Do not route P0.5 facet work through eval_firewall or vice versa. Also note: is_heldout_query has ZERO production call sites today — its only importer is freeze_heldout_eval.py:29; the checklist's automated eval-run contamination check (CHECKLIST:584-587) is deferred to P2.2 and is PLAN-ONLY. **Evidence:** grep for eval_firewall\|is_heldout_query\|firewall_active across backend/*.py returns only the module itself and freeze_heldout_eval.py — the firewall exists but nothing harvest-shaped consults it yet, exactly as CHECKLIST:586 records.
- **evaluation & gates — doc_hit:** FAILURE MODE (b): one idea implemented twice — run_heldout_eval.doc_hit and eval_metrics.exact_source_hit_at_k compute the same binary hit independently, and their semantics already diverge: doc_hit flips to ALL-expected-docs-required when the question sets expected_all_docs (run_heldout_eval.py:188-191); exact_source_hit_at_k is fixed any-hit@k with a k cutoff the heldout runner doesn't apply. Any consolidation must preserve the expected_all_docs branch or list/comparison questions will silently score easier. **Evidence:** run_heldout_eval.py:9-14 docstring defines doc_hit; eval_metrics.py:147-158 defines the twin; no cross-reference exists between the two files.
- **evaluation & gates — eval:** FAILURE MODE (b): the plan's single word 'eval' is three disjoint code harnesses that do not share scoring code. run_heldout_eval.score() re-implements recall/hit inline and never imports eval_metrics (eval_metrics' only consumer is three_tier_eval.py:14). Concretely: P1.1 box CHECKLIST:570-571 is checked [x] claiming 'nDCG/MRR' were captured, but the heldout runner computes no nDCG/MRR and EVAL_*.json summaries contain no such keys — nDCG/MRR exist only in the un-wired eval_metrics.py. The checked box overstates what the deployed scorer measures. **Evidence:** eval_metrics.py:1-6 docstring: 'intentionally not imported by the live retrieval path... belong in golden-set eval runs'; grep shows sole importer three_tier_eval.py:14. run_heldout_eval.py:182-211 computes its own doc_recall/concept_recall.
- **evaluation & gates — held-out:** Identifier drift, not concept drift: plan and critique say 56 questions ('the held-out 56', PLAN_CRITIQUE:87) but heldout_hashes.json is frozen at question_count=58 (frozen_at 2026-07-13T07:55:14Z) while all three captured EVAL_2026-07-13_*.json baselines have n=56. Two questions were added after baseline capture; any A/B against 'the 56' must subset by id or re-baseline. **Evidence:** Code spells it 'heldout' (files/functions), plan spells it 'held-out'; both denote exactly backend/evals/heldout_questions.jsonl + its frozen hashes. Shape census on disk: direct 10 (checklist:564 says 8) confirms post-freeze additions.
- **evaluation & gates — negative controls:** FAILURE MODE (a): one phrase, two different assertions — (i) 'an unanswerable question must be refused' (heldout shape + REFUSAL_RE + probe_tier_latency) and (ii) 'this vocabulary term must never be introduced by expansion' (three_tier_eval forbidden lexicon). Also mild (b): the unanswerable-question control exists three times (heldout suite, latency probe, strict-ready smoke requirement CHECKLIST:1268) with no shared question source — the tungsten query is hardcoded in the probe, not drawn from the suite's 5 negative_control rows. **Evidence:** three_tier_eval.py:605 literally says 'a negative-control vocabulary concept was introduced' — same words, vocabulary-level meaning; freeze_heldout_eval.py:89-90 enforces the answer-level meaning.
- **evaluation & gates — preregistered thresholds:** FAILURE MODE (a) adjacent: 'threshold' in code overwhelmingly means runtime production gates (ANSWERABILITY_COVERAGE_THRESHOLD, backend/services/answerability_tuning.py:97) — tuning knobs, not eval pass/fail bars; do not preregister by reusing those settings. The substantive gap: the checklist's eval acceptance criteria are qualitative ('improves materially over baseline' CHECKLIST:611, 'no held-out recall/nDCG loss' CHECKLIST:831) with no stored numbers, so today nothing is preregistered — the only pre-declared numerics anywhere are the latency budgets and the (unset) per-case min_* floors. **Evidence:** grep -riE 'preregist' across backend/ and docs/ returns nothing; ROUTE_LATENCY_BUDGETS comment (three_tier_eval.py:44-46) is the sole 'targets declared before the run' artifact.
- **evaluation & gates — regression matrix:** FAILURE MODE (b), high risk: matrix rows 1-12 (CHECKLIST:1224-1235: expert direct hit, naive bridge, single fact, broad, list, procedural, comparison, follow-up, negative control, cross-domain, cross-corpus, cross-corpus-irrelevant) are the heldout suite's shape taxonomy under different names (heldout shapes: direct, naive, single_fact, broad, list, procedural, comparison, followup, negative_control, cross_domain, cross_corpus, cross_corpus_irrelevant — freeze_heldout_eval.py:31-44). Only rows 13-16 (no-tree corpus, deleted corpus, cold-start, concurrent-ingest) are new. Building the matrix as a third standalone harness would triplicate scoring; the honest build is heldout shapes + 4 ops rows + evaluate_route_result's record contract. Also (a): 'regression suite' elsewhere means the ordinary pytest suite (docs/QUERY_PLAN_V2_RETRIEVAL.md:221) — not this matrix. **Evidence:** PLAN_CRITIQUE:52 counts the matrix as 24 open / 0 done; the live three-tier query set has only 5 queries and no negative-control/forbidden_lexicon_terms row despite the validator supporting one (three_tier_eval.py:328-337).
- **evaluation & gates — scorer:** FAILURE MODE (a): 'scorer' elsewhere in the codebase means production RANKING scorers — services/retriever/lexical.py:49 (fallback lexical scorer), services/retriever/evidence_allocation.py:13,66 (metadata-aware scorer), services/graph/orchestrator.py:2715 (graph ranking-signal scorer). Same word, retrieval-time concept, never an eval concept. FAILURE MODE (b): three parallel eval scorers (score(), evaluate_route_result, evaluate_case) with no shared code. Hygiene bug: the in-file comment still says 'scorer v2' (run_heldout_eval.py:45) while HEAD is v3 (commit e1d4a4f) and artifacts stamp scorer_version=3 — the comment is the kind of stale tag PLAN_CRITIQUE C4 warns about. **Evidence:** git log on run_heldout_eval.py: f2fb6e2 (v1) -> 7be5a53 (v2) -> e1d4a4f (v3, offline rescore, rescored_v3=true rows); EVAL_2026-07-13_qdrant_only.json:88 'scorer_version': 3.
- **extraction & parity — burst orchestration:** (a) 'burst' is used for three unrelated things: extraction bursts (this row), embed bursts (embedder.py:8,62), and upload-admission bursts ('500-file simultaneous burst', ingestion/admission.py:12) plus incidental 'burst-load DNS blip' in llm.py. (b) build-vs-built split inside P2.7b itself: account routing/failover is DONE; disposition matrix, chunk-complete barrier, eligible-chunk manifest, per-burst chunks/sec + worker-seconds + cost-per-1k-chunks recording have NO code — grep 'barrier\|manifest\|disposition' in backend/services/ingestion returns nothing relevant. Nearest seam for the barrier: worker.py pass_plan/batch machinery (batches.py INGEST_PROFILES) which already sequences queryable-vs-enrichment passes. **Evidence:** checklist P2.7b bullets vs code: only bullet 'dispatch routes request batches across enabled accounts' is checked [x]; batches.py:137 runpod_burst profile has no barrier semantics.
- **extraction & parity — disposition matrix:** No code symbol, no Mongo field. It is a GATE artifact, not a config: S3 (PLAN_CRITIQUE:82) produces it from the heading/OCR audit and it must precede S4's paid pass and S11's burst. Nearest existing seams: (1) per-corpus engine stamp resolved by resolve_extraction_contract (extraction_contract.py:164 — corpus-level extraction config already exists and is the natural home for a disposition field), (2) corpus strict-readiness (services/ingestion/readiness.py) which P2.7 already plans to wire extraction/concept readiness into. Risk (b): teams could encode disposition twice — once as a doc/table for sign-off and again ad hoc as engine toggles; make the corpus settings field the single source and render the sign-off table from it. **Evidence:** grep -rni disposition backend --include=*.py = zero hits; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:82-83 and checklist P2.7b bullet 1 define it only in prose.
- **extraction & parity — engine parity:** (a) 'parity' means three unrelated things in this repo: 1) P2.6/P2.7 engine/extraction parity (this row — measurement harness is PLAN-ONLY, no comparison script exists); 2) Mongo<->Qdrant eligible-ID parity in lexicon backfill (backend/scripts/backfill_corpus_lexicon.py:516, corpus_lexicon.py:2734); 3) runpod-EMBED cosine parity promotion gate (P1.9, checklist line ~790: local-mxfp8 vs remote-fp = 0.98). When the plan says 'parity compare' in P2.7 it means engine-vs-engine yield/quality on the SAME chunks — nothing shipped does that today. **Evidence:** grep -rni parity backend --include=*.py returns only Mongo/Qdrant and score-parity hits; no engine-comparison harness exists in backend/scripts.
- **extraction & parity — ghost_b:** (a) one word, two scopes: 'ghost_b' names BOTH the whole extraction stage (any engine writes ghost_b_extractions — including runpod_flash) AND specifically services/ghost_b.py's provider-LLM lane. The engine enum makes this concrete: engine='local' now means a private provider LLM running ghost_b.py's contract, while the old GLiNER/GLiREL sidecar is 'legacy_local' (extraction_contract.py:15-19) — the word 'local' silently changed meaning across the rename. **Evidence:** worker.py:1923-1928 comments enumerate the engines; runpod_flash_extraction.py returns 'the same validated wire shape as ghost_b_local' (module docstring) and all engines persist to ghost_b_extractions.
- **extraction & parity — GLiNER:** (a) 'GLiNER' means two different models in two places: local lane = entity-only GLiNER + separate GLiREL for relations; RunPod lane = gliner-relex joint model doing both. P2.7b's 'GLiNER-ReLEX + spaCy windows... in the worker' is the remote stack; the local stack is a DIFFERENT composition producing the same artifact shape — this is exactly the parity surface P2.6/P2.7 must measure, not an accidental duplicate. **Evidence:** ghost_b_local.py:9-16 (two GLiNER passes + GLiREL) vs runpod_flash_extractor/app.py:312,607 (single relex model, entity-only execution path note); test_runpod_flash_extraction.py:113 asserts output_mode=='joint_relex'.
- **extraction & parity — GLiREL:** (b) duplicate-implementation risk (intentional, must stay measured): relation extraction exists twice — GLiREL locally vs gliner-relex remotely (and a third time via provider-LLM RelationItems in ghost_b.py). GLiREL is NOT shipped in the RunPod worker (app.py deps list gliner+spacy only, app.py:538-539), so 'burst runs the full local stack remotely' (P2.7b) is currently false in the strict sense — the burst runs the RELEX stack, not the GLiNER+GLiREL stack. **Evidence:** runpod_flash_extractor/app.py:538-546 pip deps contain gliner==0.2.27 and spacy but no glirel; ghost_b_local.py:262-263 imports gliner AND glirel for in-process mode.
- **extraction & parity — negation:** (a) 'negation' already means something else in shipped code: retriever-side negated-query-concept filtering (query_plan.py/vocabulary.py). P2.4's negation is extraction-side and has NO code counterpart. Nearest seams: RelationItem (ghost_b.py:1988) for the negated field, _EVIDENCE_STOPWORDS/_evidence_token_overlap (ghost_b.py:682,690) for token preservation, and the worker's spaCy parse (app.py:292) for evidence-sentence boundaries. **Evidence:** grep 'negated' in backend/services: only ghost_b.py:682 comment + retriever hits; RelationItem field list has no negated.
- **extraction & parity — provenance:** (a) one word, four shipped mechanisms plus one planned taxonomy: plan sentences using bare 'provenance' must be resolved per-context — P0.1/P0.8 mean extractor/summary_model stamps, P2.8 means lexicon source-ID lists, P2.1/P2.2 mean the FOUR-CLASS taxonomy (extracted/derived/attested/generated) which is PLAN-ONLY (grep provenance_class = zero hits; nearest seam = corpus_lexicon entry builder + _lexicon_payload). Risk (b): the four-class field could get implemented separately on lexicon entries, Qdrant representation points, and cards; it should be one enum defined once (corpus_lexicon) and projected. **Evidence:** checklist:864-866 defines the four classes; card_builder.py:34 'provenance contract' and qdrant_writer.py:1448 comment show the two shipped projection surfaces.
- **extraction & parity — relations:** (a) one word, four layers (wire relation / durable artifact / graph edge / lexicon projection) — all the same concept flowing downstream, but line-item plan language like 'relation artifact' (P2.4) means the ghost_b_extractions row, not the Neo4j edge. Separately, P2.1 PLANS 'associated_concepts' (co-occurrence associations) and explicitly orders them kept DISTINCT from typed factual relations — associated_concepts has zero code today; do not implement it as another relations[] field. **Evidence:** checklist P2.1 'Keep these associations distinct from typed factual graph relations'; grep associated_concepts/usage_frames/semantic_profile in backend = no hits.
- **extraction & parity — RunPod:** (a) 'RunPod' names two lanes with different contracts and different workers: extraction (runpod_flash_extractor/, polymath.runpod_gliner_relex.vN) and embedding (runpod_flash_embedder/, polymath.runpod_embed.v1, embedder.py mode='runpod'). NOT a duplicate implementation — the embed lane deliberately imports _AccountDispatcher and _submit_and_wait from runpod_flash_extraction.py (embedder.py:581), so routing/polling exists once. Keep it that way: any P2.7b dispatch changes land in runpod_flash_extraction.py and both lanes inherit. **Evidence:** embedder.py:566-581 ('the enabled account with the least in-flight batches wins... services.runpod_flash_extraction._AccountDispatcher').
- **extraction & parity — spaCy:** None serious, but note the asymmetry: spaCy exists ONLY in the RunPod worker; the local GLiREL lane does its own sentence windowing without spaCy (glirel_infer.py). 'spaCy windows' in P2.7b describes the remote stack only. **Evidence:** grep 'spacy' across backend hits only runpod_flash_extraction.py:551 and schemas.py:641; worker app.py:292-299 is the sole loader.
- **extraction & parity — typed signatures:** (b) HIGH duplicate-implementation risk: P2.5 reads as green-field ('build a reviewed compatibility table', 'annotate signature_valid') but the table and enforcement ALREADY exist under the domain_range name — and current behavior is STRONGER than the plan wants (it remaps/warns; P2.5 says annotate-only, no drop/remap). Implementing signature_valid beside _apply_domain_range would double the mechanism. Correct move: evolve DOMAIN_RANGE_MAP into the reviewed table and demote remap to annotation, one mechanism. **Evidence:** ghost_b.py:2734-2760 remap path; P2.5 'Initially annotate signature_valid and violation reason; do not drop or remap edges automatically' — direct contradiction with the deployed remap-to-related_to behavior.
- **extraction & parity — utility gloss:** (a) THREE-way word collision: 1) 'utility' is also a query-MODEL KIND (settings.py:739, chat_orchestrator.py:6974 resolve_query_model_kind(user_id,'utility')) — a cheap-LLM role, nothing to do with glosses; 2) 'gloss' is also UNIVERSAL_ENTITY_GLOSSES (ghost_b.py:217-223) — prompt-only entity-type disambiguation glosses, explicitly 'not consumed by any storage code'; 3) siblings retrieval_gloss/embedding_gloss are different fields — the checklist's 'one compromise gloss vector' critique (line ~892) refers to embedding_gloss being the single embedded surface, and the planned representation_method='utility' point (line 911) would embed utility_gloss content as its own vector. representation_method itself is PLAN-ONLY (only test fixture mentions of utility_gloss exist outside these files). **Evidence:** grep representation_method in backend = tests only; qdrant_writer.py:1452-1454 shows all three gloss fields side by side.
- **extraction & parity — wire contract:** (a) 'contract' is overloaded FOUR ways in this family: 1) this wire contract (runpod_gliner_relex.vN, request/response shape); 2) the embed wire contract polymath.runpod_embed.v1 (embedder.py:63-67, different worker); 3) the durable ARTIFACT schema polymath.extract.v1/v2 (ghost_b_extractions.schema_version — survives in Mongo, wire does not); 4) ExtractionContract (extraction_contract.py:82 — which-engine-runs resolution, no wire at all). Also note the deliberate name drift time_expressions (wire) vs temporal_captures (artifact): SAME concept, two names on either side of _validate_wire_result — cite both when auditing T-HOOK-2 backfills. **Evidence:** runpod_flash_extraction.py:414-419 performs the rename; PLAN_CRITIQUE:67 confirms the fields 'exist only in runpod_flash_extraction.py (T-HOOK-1 wire capture)'.
- **facets & lenses — agency_preservation:** Identical id in plan and code. Multiword aliases ('self determination', 'value alignment', 'personal meaning') still pass the one-word specificity gate, so unlike emotional_patterns this facet CAN still be stamped on new ingests — S7 cannot be treated as legacy-only cleanup for this id. **Evidence:** normalizer.py:225-236; _specific_content_alias (:632) only rejects len(words)==1 aliases.
- **facets & lenses — broad-alias family:** FAILURE MODE (b): broad-alias suppression is already implemented TWICE — ingest-side _specific_content_alias (normalizer.py:632) and query-side SINGLE_TOKEN_ALIAS_DETECTION_KEYS (query_semantics.py:490); S7's DF rule would be a third mechanism. They gate different stages (stamping vs query alias detection vs retrieval rights) but the plan should say so explicitly or one will be rebuilt as the other. Also note _specific_content_alias only blocks ONE-WORD aliases — multiword broad aliases (e.g. 'self determination') still stamp, and the 89k/38k historical stamps remain in stores. **Evidence:** PLAN_CRITIQUE:69 'the family the P0.5 decontamination deliberately left stamps 71-100% of every corpus, i.e. DF-worthless'; checklist:472 acceptance 'No broad incidental word stamps unrelated domain facets' still unchecked.
- **facets & lenses — content_facet_ids:** DISTINCT from facet_ids by design and must stay so: facet_ids is the retrievable handle (headings + gated inheritance), content_facet_ids is own-text evidence. p0_5 decontamination explicitly 'Never touches content_facet_ids' (p0_5_facet_decontamination.py:38). Sub-field 'content_facet_source' (values chunk_text/parent_summary/child_text/parent_text/document_content, normalizer.py:709) is part of the 'source' word overload — see source_level row. **Evidence:** normalizer.py:649-660 docstring: 'soft retrieval hints ... avoid model calls'; p0_5 script:81-86 uses them as the evidence side of the contamination rule.
- **facets & lenses — DF / document frequency:** FAILURE MODE (b) risk: three DF-shaped stats could get built in parallel — (1) p0_5 parent_df (facet-level, computed ad hoc), (2) Qdrant server-side IDF (term-level, already live for BM25), (3) planned P2.1 DF/specificity fields and S7 threshold rule. S7 and P2.1 should both consume ONE persisted facet-DF stat derived from the p0_5 audit code path (audit_corpus:177-236 is the nearest existing seam; batch its cleanup backfill with S5 reindex per PLAN_CRITIQUE:86). Note checklist:156 'measure facet DF per corpus' is DONE (audit exists); the ADMISSION RULE is not. **Evidence:** p0_5 script:230 '"parent_df": df'; grep for df_floor/document_frequency/term admission in backend returns nothing else.
- **facets & lenses — emotional_patterns:** Plan and code use the identical id. Member of the broad-alias family (S7 decision pending). Every alias in its list is one word, so under the current _specific_content_alias gate the facet can effectively no longer be stamped by alias match at all — the id survives only in legacy stored data. **Evidence:** normalizer.py:237; PLAN_CRITIQUE:65 table row 'Parents w/ facet in {emotional_patterns, agency_preservation}'.
- **facets & lenses — facet:** FAILURE MODE (a): one word, two runtime concepts. 'Facet' = (1) stored ingest metadata (facets/normalizer.py, polymath.facets.v1) and (2) query-side coverage lanes (_CHAT_COVERAGE_FACETS chat_orchestrator.py:239, FacetCandidate.lanes final_selector.py:51). They meet only through matching_ingest_facets/matching_vector_facets. FAILURE MODE (b): _CHAT_COVERAGE_FACETS trigger/support-term rows duplicate the same concepts as _CONTENT_FACET_ALIASES (normalizer.py:167) — on_device_llm, privacy, knowledge_graph appear verbatim in BOTH hand-maintained tables (chat_orchestrator.py:241/279/307 vs normalizer.py:296/338/168); editing one silently diverges the other. **Evidence:** normalizer.py:1-7 states facets give docs/chunks 'a stable semantic handle'; final_selector.py:1-9 says selector consumes 'facet/lane tags'; runtime.py:1-7 'metadata-only' helpers bridge the two.
- **facets & lenses — facet contamination:** FAILURE MODE (a): 'contamination' also names an unrelated concept in the same checklist: held-out EVAL contamination (backend/services/eval_firewall.py:1 'contamination firewall', backend/scripts/freeze_heldout_eval.py:10, checklist P1.1 line 578/895). A grep or a checklist row saying just 'contamination' conflates data-leak-into-eval with lens-leak-into-facets. Status nuance: decontamination is merged/live (checklist:158, 68,943 parents + 271 docs) but PLAN_CRITIQUE:109 notes it deliberately LEFT the broad-alias family stamped — 'facet contamination' in the critique now mostly refers to that residue, which is a different mechanism (hand-authored aliases, not lens inheritance). **Evidence:** checklist:118 'corpus-lens facet contamination is real (68% of sampled ecommerce parents)'; p0_5 script docstring lines 19-45.
- **facets & lenses — facet_ids:** FAILURE MODE (a): the key 'facet_ids' changes meaning by carrier — on a chunk row it is the chunk's list; inside documents.facet_profile it is the document's list, which is then RENAMED to doc_facet_ids when copied onto Qdrant payloads (worker.py:2926 doc_facet_ids = facet_profile['facet_ids']). Any consumer that greps for 'facet_ids' without knowing the carrier gets the wrong scope. Recommend: plan text should always say chunk facet_ids vs doc_facet_ids. **Evidence:** worker.py:2926 `doc_facet_ids = (facet_profile or {}).get("facet_ids")`; normalizer.py:927 doc list stored under plain 'facet_ids'; runtime.py:107-131 handles both keys side by side.
- **facets & lenses — lens:** FAILURE MODE (a): 'lens' has three meanings in this codebase: (1) SchemaLens extraction guidance (intended role, ghost_b.py:1072 docstring: 'guides extraction prompts; never creates permanent schema labels'); (2) the lens-as-facet-source misuse that P0.5 decontaminated (normalizer.py:476); (3) the chat retrieval-synthesis lens (chat_orchestrator.py:5524), a prompt-shaping construct with zero relation to facets. Plan text saying bare 'lens' in a retrieval section is ambiguous between (2) and (3). Always qualify: schema_lens vs synthesis lens. **Evidence:** SCHEMA_LENS_VERSION vs FACET_SCHEMA_VERSION are separate schemas; chat_orchestrator.py:5548 emits literal tag <retrieval_synthesis_lens>.
- **facets & lenses — source_level:** FAILURE MODE (a): the 'source' word family is 5-way overloaded around facets: (1) source_level (facet provenance, this row); (2) content_facet_source (which text produced content facets: chunk_text/parent_summary/child_text/parent_text/document_content, normalizer.py:709); (3) semantic_facets.source='ingestion' (normalizer.py:723); (4) coverage-facet 'source' = ingest_facet_profile \| vector_facet_probe (runtime.py:281,470); (5) source_tier / source_refs (document provenance). Checklist P0.5 'Add artifact diagnostics for facet source, confidence, and evidence' (:467, unchecked) must specify WHICH — recommend it surface source_level AND content_facet_source under those exact names. **Evidence:** normalizer.py:470 writes the field; runtime.py:425 reads it; no other consumer in backend (grep source_level = 7 hits, all facets/*).
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — abstractive summary:** Defined negatively in code: abstractive = NOT an extractive-fallback and NOT an empty-model placeholder. The positive marker is the summary_model provenance field. **Evidence:** qdrant_writer.py:847-866; checklist:60-61 invariant; checklist:1322-1334.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — acceptance tests:** Ledger 'acceptance' includes durable-data probes and live probes, not just pytest; many acceptance lines have no automated counterpart yet (e.g. the 16x3 regression matrix, checklist:52) — those are to-be-built, nearest seam three_tier_eval/run_heldout_eval. **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:34-36; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:52 (regression matrix row).
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — api keys:** Invariant (checklist:67-68) is about never printing/committing them; enforcement is convention + encryption at the settings boundary, no scanner exists in-repo. **Evidence:** settings.py:253-366,487-521; checklist:1090.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — baseline commit:** COLLISION (a): 'baseline' means three things — (1) this git ref (checklist:4), (2) the durable-data baseline captured by backend/scripts/capture_raptor_baseline.py into docs/baselines/BASELINE_2026-07-13.json ('Current Durable Baseline' section, checklist:75-95), (3) the held-out eval baseline (docs/baselines/EVAL_2026-07-13_*.json, S8 A/B 'vs the 2026-07-13 baseline'). Always qualify which baseline. **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:4; backend/scripts/capture_raptor_baseline.py:1-12 docstring; docs/baselines/ contains BASELINE_2026-07-13.json, EVAL_2026-07-13_{qdrant_only,qdrant_mongo,qdrant_mongo_graph}.json, LATENCY_2026-07-13.json.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — capture hook:** T-HOOK-3 (docling publication-vs-file-date de-conflation, S2) has NO code counterpart yet — to-be-built; nearest seam backend/services/ingestion/docling_adapter.py. Fields existing in the contract is NOT data coverage: plan-verified 0 rows carry temporal_class. **Evidence:** PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:67,80-83,100; grep temporal_class → only ghost_a.py + runpod_flash_extraction.py.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — capture-before-rebuild:** No code enforcement exists — the rule is prose in the ledger + repo CLAUDE.md north-star. Nearest seam for a guard: the rebuild entry points backend/scripts/build_librarian_cards.py, backend/scripts/backfill_corpus_lexicon.py, backend/services/ingestion/summary_backfill.py. **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:16-19; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:112; CLAUDE.md:9-33 (north-star: rebuild freeze while adopted capture hooks unbuilt).
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — claim audit:** No code; the auditable-claims machinery is capture_raptor_baseline.py + the verify subcommands (nearest seam), and session Task #17 'Exec-plan audit workflow: every checklist claim -> auditable action' is the unbuilt automation. Related but distinct docs: RAPTOR_CRITIQUE_RESPONSE_2026-07-12.md (maintainer response) and PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md (sequencing critique). **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:97-121 (Audit Delta 1+2); P0.7 'doc/claim integrity' COMPLETE (PLAN_CRITIQUE:25).
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — corpus boundary:** No single 'boundary' symbol; the invariant is enforced at three layers (filter threading, per-corpus collections, graph properties). A test asserting the invariant end-to-end does not exist as one artifact — checklist:50 remains open. **Evidence:** planned_fusion.py:303,398; qdrant_writer.py:168-176; checklist:50.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — corpus_id:** Note Neo4j uses the PLURAL corpus_ids[] (shared entities across corpora) — same identity, different cardinality by store. **Evidence:** qdrant_writer.py:939; card_builder.py:21.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — dark fields:** 'Dark fields' (captured field without consumer) vs 'dark code' (merged code not deployed, S0/C3) are sibling but distinct hygiene failures — the plan uses both; don't collapse them. **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:20-22; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:68,79,105-106 (C3).
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — deterministic policy:** Spans two layers (retriever reservation + librarian roles) — see the 'document seats' collision row; the invariant text (checklist:69-73) covers both. **Evidence:** reservation_policy.py:19; shelf_engine.py:192; checklist:69-73.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — document seats:** COLLISION (a) + duplicate-implementation risk (b): two seat systems at different granularity. S8 wires shelf_reserve THROUGH reservation_policy ('never the old unconditional floor') — building librarian seats as a second independent reservation path is the explicitly-banned duplicate. **Evidence:** reservation_policy.py:1-19; shelf_engine.py:192; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:71,87 (S8).
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — durable leases:** COLLISION (a): one plan term, two implementations with different semantics and test surfaces. The P0.6 open 'lease test matrix' (restart-before-expiry, purge-beyond-lease) targets the CLEANUP lease only — a green lane-lease suite proves nothing about it. **Evidence:** job_leases.py:39-207; ingestion_service.py:1954; checklist:24 (P0.6 row), 104-105.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — ecommerce_ai_film_school:** Name-case drift: harvested term is lowercase but the store name is mixed-case — case-sensitive Mongo queries with the lowercase form return nothing. **Evidence:** cleanup_orphan_catalog.py:32; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:5.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — embedder /health:** DUPLICATE-IMPLEMENTATION (b): TWO embedder services exist — repo /embedder (docker, NO warmup code) and scripts/apple_ml_services/embedder_mlx (host MLX :8082, has P1.8 warmup). The deployed one is embedder_mlx (docker-compose.override routes EMBEDDER_URL to host :8082). Checklist line 93-95 ('Embedder /health ... does not certify a completed inference warmup') predates the merged warmup — now stale per checklist:746-761 receipts. **Evidence:** embedder_mlx/main.py:11,49-51,134-140; checklist:746-761 (merged @7f8e7b2, live :8082/health receipts); grep warmup embedder/main.py -> no hits.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — execution plan:** The ledger is item truth, the plan file is its ORDERING (checklist:8-12) — two docs, one plan-of-record; do not treat them as independent plans. **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:6-12 'Execution Order — SEQUENCED PLAN OF RECORD ... docs/PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md (S0-S14)'; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:73-93 (S0-S14 table).
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — experiment:** No experiment framework exists in code; the before/after harness is run_heldout_eval.py + docs/baselines artifacts — nearest seam for P3.4/P3.5 experiments. **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:43; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:49-50,92.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — extractive fallback:** DUPLICATE-IMPLEMENTATION (b): the same idea is implemented twice under near-identical names — ghost_a._extractive_fallback_summary (parents) and summary_tree._extractive_fallback (tree nodes). Per-seam duplication is currently intentional, but checklist:955 ('never create extractive fallback placeholders after provider failure') must be enforced at BOTH sites; fixing one will not fix the other. **Evidence:** ghost_a.py:349; summary_tree.py:380; config.py:321.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — fail closed:** TWO mirror gates share the knobs (services/answerability_tuning.py): keep retriever gate STRICT, tune chat gate — loosening the wrong one breaks either fail-closed or answer coverage. **Evidence:** answerability_tuning.py:6-7; chat_orchestrator.py:950; checklist:64,1359.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — fast:** 'Fast' is a human label that exists only in docs/probe names; code always says qdrant_only. Note test_planned_retrieval.py:225 'planned fast' = QueryPlanV2 over qdrant_only — same tier, planned route. **Evidence:** probe_tier_latency.py:13,190; checklist:48-49,91.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — final evidence:** No `final_evidence` symbol; three modules co-own finalization (ranking_policy, reservation_policy, final_selector). Checklist:479 'no separator/navigation artifact can enter a final evidence packet' is still open — nearest seam is the NOISY_KINDS junk filter. **Evidence:** reservation_policy.py:1-19; ranking_policy.py:1717,1855; checklist:56,479.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — graph:** COLLISION (a), strong: 'graph' = (1) the tier, (2) the Neo4j subsystem, (3) the _graph Qdrant collection kind, (4) Graph View frontend. Plan text usually means (1); code greps for 'graph' hit all four. **Evidence:** _schemas_legacy.py:1073; graph/orchestrator.py:3203 requested_tier='qdrant_mongo_graph'; qdrant_writer.py:172 kinds {naive, hrag, graph, schemas}.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — hardcoded topic rules:** The term names an ANTI-pattern: code exists (query_semantics curated lists) and the plan's work is its removal/migration (checklist:62-63 invariant; P0.5 'curated aliases -> versioned lexicon'; S5 registry). Grepping for a 'topic_rules' symbol finds nothing — the rules hide inside query_semantics constants. **Evidence:** query_semantics.py:848; checklist:62-63; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:84.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — hybrid:** COLLISION (a): 'hybrid' ALSO means dense+sparse hybrid vector search inside Qdrant (qdrant_writer.py:958 'named=... sparse=...') — a scoring mechanism orthogonal to the store tier. A 'hybrid' result can come from any tier. **Evidence:** backend/models/_schemas_legacy.py:1072; probe_tier_latency.py:191; qdrant_writer.py:958.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — idempotent replay:** No single symbol; the invariant (checklist:65-66) is a property each job family implements separately (summary jobs, extraction jobs, graph promotion, lexicon slices). checklist:502 adds partial-stage retry to the same family. **Evidence:** batches.py:6,441; graph_backfill.py:682; corpus_lexicon.py:2785.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — inference warmup:** Distinct from P1.9 'keepalive after startup warmup' (checklist:809) and Qdrant compile/inference warmup cost (checklist:816) — same word 'warmup', different subsystems; P1.9 items remain unbuilt. **Evidence:** embedder_mlx/main.py:49-51; reranker_mlx/main.py:299; checklist:743-761,809,816; PLAN_CRITIQUE:79 (S0 'merge wave1/warm').
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — latency probes:** The checklist's 'Recent deployed latency probes: Fast 39.8s degraded...' (line 91) are this script's outputs; P1.9 per-stage timing is a separate, unbuilt finer-grained instrument. **Evidence:** probe_tier_latency.py:1-8; docs/baselines/LATENCY_2026-07-13.json; checklist:91.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — ledger:** None — 'ledger' and 'implementation ledger' are one artifact; the checklist self-declares it. **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:34 'This file is the implementation ledger'; :11 'This ledger stays the item-level source of truth'. Not code — a governance doc.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — migration:** COLLISION (a): one word covers (1) the Status Legend tag, (2) the executed backfill scripts, (3) the pending Neo4j edge migration (S12) and Qdrant collection consolidation (P3.3). Standing rule 4 'one migration per seam' governs (3). In code the executed form is almost always called 'backfill', not 'migration' — name drift. **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:44,26-27; qdrant_writer.py:125; ls backend/scripts \| grep backfill (15 hits).
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — owner waiver:** Pure process; no code or store records a waiver. If waivers matter, the ledger is the only ledger of them. **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:18; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:82,86 (S3/S7 owner decisions).
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — partial:** COLLISION (a): 'partial' also appears as partial-stage retry (checklist:502, P0.6 job semantics) and as the planned temporal capability level 'temporal_partial' (S6). Tag vs mechanism vs capability level. **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:42,502; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:85 (S6 temporal_unavailable/partial/strict_ready).
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — plan critique:** COLLISION (a): 'critique' also names a DIFFERENT 2026-07-12 artifact set — docs/RAPTOR_RAG_CRITIQUE_AND_ROADMAP.md + RAPTOR_CRITIQUE_RESPONSE_2026-07-12.md + RAPTOR_CRITIQUE_ACKNOWLEDGEMENT_2026-07-12.md (external review + maintainer response). The 2026-07-13 plan critique is a separate sequencing audit. Cite the date. **Evidence:** PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:97-109 (C1-C4); docs/RAPTOR_CRITIQUE_RESPONSE_2026-07-12.md:1-5.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — polymath_v2:** 'v2' shorthand in the plan always means this corpus, not schema polymath.extract.v2 — beware when grepping. **Evidence:** cleanup_orphan_catalog.py:31; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:5,59.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — pressure gates:** Two modules (pressure.py = pipeline/provider, storage_pressure.py = Qdrant memory) — complementary, not duplicates; the invariant (checklist:65-66) requires BOTH active during backfills. **Evidence:** pressure.py:121; storage_pressure.py:91,210; worker.py:3657.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — qdrant collections:** P3.3 'collection consolidation' plans to change this layout — a migration-tagged item; until then _col_for_corpus is the only legal resolver. **Evidence:** qdrant_writer.py:125,168-176; config.py:645; checklist:87-88,506.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — read-before-act:** No enforcement hook exists; the rule lives in checklist:28-30 and CLAUDE.md north-star. Nearest seam if automated: a Claude Code hook that re-opens the checklist at phase start. **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:28-30; CLAUDE.md:9-14.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — reranker readiness:** The DEPLOYED reranker per user memory is the torch fp16 sidecar on :8081 (plist-managed) while reranker_mlx is the rollback backend — verify which binary serves :8081 before trusting readiness semantics. Repo also contains a /reranker docker service with NO warmup (grep empty) — third implementation of 'the reranker'. **Evidence:** reranker_mlx/main.py:297-310; checklist:93 'Reranker readiness includes real startup inference'; grep warmup reranker/main.py -> no hits.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — retrieval tiers:** COLLISION (a): 'tier' also names Tier-0 doc-summary routing (config.py TIER0_ROUTING — a routing stage, not a RetrievalTier) and SourceChunk.source_tier (per-chunk provenance). See registry_retrieval.json 'tier' row. **Evidence:** backend/models/_schemas_legacy.py:1068; backend/scripts/probe_tier_latency.py:190-204 maps fast/hybrid/graph labels onto the enum values.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — rollups:** Do not confuse with S5 'alias/latent rollup' (PLAN_CRITIQUE:84) — that is a DIFFERENT, to-be-built aggregation (parent latent_concepts -> doc profile -> lexicon), sharing only the word. Flag when reading S5. **Evidence:** summary_tree.py:447-483; checklist:86,387.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — s0-s14:** Step ids exist only in the plan doc; session Tasks #18/#19 mirror S1/S3 by name — keep ids in sync when the plan is re-sequenced. **Evidence:** PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:77-93 rows S0..S14; checklist:10 'Execute in S-order'.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — schema version:** One field name, many independent version namespaces — that is by design; the invariant (checklist:58-59) requires it on every generated artifact family. **Evidence:** test_contracts.py:75-87; ghost_a.py:90; card_builder.py:26.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — seam:** Not a code symbol; each named seam resolves to a concrete contract module. Keep the plan's seam→file mapping explicit or 'one migration per seam' is unenforceable. **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:18,26-27; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:80 (S1 'same seam as latent_concepts').
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — semantic drift:** No symbol named 'drift'; the invariant (checklist:54-55) is realized by lane protection plus S5's 'gated query-expansion consumption (original-query lane protected)'. Same concept as the 'original query' row. **Evidence:** checklist:54-55; retriever/__init__.py:1445; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:84 (S5).
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — session memory:** Do not confuse with mem0/agent memory systems elsewhere in the user's stack — here it strictly means an agent acting on its recollection of the ledger instead of the file on disk (the C1/C2 root cause). **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:29-30; CLAUDE.md:13-14 ('agent worked from its session memory of the plan instead of the file').
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — stale:** COLLISION (a): ledger-'stale' (a status tag contradicted by a green verify, e.g. P0.1's two IN PROGRESS tags) vs code-'stale' (cache/epoch staleness, provider_canary_cache). Same word, unrelated mechanisms. **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:23-25; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:19,70,108-109 (C4); backend/services/ingestion/provider_canary_cache.py.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — status tag:** 'IN CODE' is used in the critique table but is NOT defined in the Status Legend (checklist:38-44) — undefined tag in the vocabulary; worth adding to the legend. **Evidence:** RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md:23-25,38-44; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:9 table header 'IN CODE / PARTIAL'.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — summary points:** Distinct from Tier-0 doc-summary vectors in polymath_doc_summaries (doc-level, config.py:645) — 'summary point' in the durable-baseline counts (checklist:77-84) means the parent-level points. **Evidence:** qdrant_writer.py:109,954-958; capture_raptor_baseline.py:10 'per-corpus summary-point totals'.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — top-down retrieval:** The invariant (checklist:51-52) is broader than any one flag: TIER0_ROUTING covers doc routing; shelf routing (P1.4) and tree descent (Phase 2) are separate unbuilt/partial pieces of the same invariant. Duplicate-implementation risk (b): Tier-0 routing and P1.4 shelf routing both answer 'which documents first' — S-plan must wire shelf routing THROUGH Tier-0, not beside it. **Evidence:** config.py:650-656; test_planned_retrieval.py:231; checklist:51-52.
- **harvested terms not in any seeded family (leftover) — ugo_corpus:** Same case-drift hazard as ecommerce_AI_FILM_SCHOOL. **Evidence:** cleanup_orphan_catalog.py:34; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:5,83.
- **hierarchy & headers — boundaries:** Failure mode (a), worst in family: one word, at least four implementations plus one PLAN-ONLY (evidence_spans exact boundaries, P2.1). P0.1's open verify item and P0.2's open fixture item both say 'boundaries' but target different stores (summary provenance vs tree node extents); implementing one validator for both would be wrong, implementing two under the same name invites failure mode (b). **Evidence:** grep -rn boundar backend/services returns ghost_a prompt, chunker splitters, schema_validators architectural sense, and answerability_tuning.py:116 coverage-boundary — all unrelated.
- **hierarchy & headers — child chunk:** Failure mode (a): 'child' spans three id spaces — child_chunk ids (chunk_id), summary provenance source_child_ids (P0.1 'verify summary has child IDs' means THESE), and tree child_node_ids (P0.2 'one-child section' means THESE, they are rollup node ids). The Mongo collection for child chunks is named plain 'chunks', hiding the parent/child asymmetry (parent_chunks vs chunks). **Evidence:** P0.1 checklist line 367 vs P0.2 line 385 use 'child' for different stores; contracts.py:128 chunk_type enum child\|summary\|doc_summary.
- **hierarchy & headers — doc summary:** Failure mode (a): 'summary' spans two levels — parent summaries (chunk_type='summary', Funnel A breadth, Ghost A parent_summary.v1) vs document summaries (chunk_type='doc_summary', Tier-0 routing only, '§10.2: routing card, NEVER evidence'). The owner-architecture line 'doc_summaries = only summary vectors' reads ambiguously against the live schema where parent-summary vectors dominate the naive collection. Plan phrase 'document summary tree' (checklist:1239) means the whole per-doc summary_tree, a third referent. **Evidence:** tier0.py:2-10 docstring states the card/evidence split; contracts.py:128 keeps the two as distinct enum values.
- **hierarchy & headers — heading_path:** Failure mode (a): the typed-contract layer (contracts.ChunkMetadata) calls the identical concept section_path while every store and dataclass calls it heading_path; the P0.8 typed-writer-boundary work (open) will collide these two names at the writer seam if not reconciled. **Evidence:** contracts.py:107 literally comments '# heading_path' next to section_path; live probe (PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md section 2) shows heading_path non-empty on 86-100% of parents per corpus.
- **hierarchy & headers — hierarchy descent:** Partial: descent over Tier-0-routed documents is BUILT; the Phase-2 item 'Descend reserved documents through tree -> parent -> child evidence' (checklist:300) — descent driven from shelf_reserve seats — is not, because shelf_reserve itself has no code symbol (PLAN_CRITIQUE section 2). Wiring risk: implementing seat-descent as a new path instead of feeding reserved doc_ids into the existing SummaryTreeNavigator would be failure mode (b). **Evidence:** summary_tree_navigator.py:503 names the strategy; retriever/__init__.py:2225 comment 'derived probes may narrow through Tier-0 and the RAPTOR tree'.
- **hierarchy & headers — mid-document header:** If this term denotes headers appearing mid-flow that should split/route chunks, that behavior already exists across THREE seams (synthetic injection pre-parse, docling section_heading blocks, page-heading demotion in the tree). A new 'mid-document header' feature built fresh would be failure mode (b); it should be specified as a change to one of these seams. The open plan work nearest to it is P0.5 'Audit OCR-corrupt heading paths and define a source-safe repair rule' (checklist:462) and plan S3's heading/OCR quality audit. **Evidence:** grep -rniE 'mid.document' over docs/, CONTINUITY/, backend/, frontend/ returns zero hits.
- **hierarchy & headers — page_start:** Failure mode (b) variant — implemented-but-dead: the field, writers, and readers all exist, but live data is 0 populated on all four corpora (PLAN_CRITIQUE section 2 table; checklist Audit Delta 2 'parent page_start/entity char offsets are 0'). Plan S3 codifies it: 'page_start is absent everywhere and only a reingest lane can ever add it'. Any new plan item that 'adds page anchors' would duplicate this existing plumbing instead of feeding it. **Evidence:** Only the ocr_ast branch (tier_chunker.py:1429-1473) passes non-None values; heading-bound tiers A/B/B+ never set it.
- **hierarchy & headers — parent chunk:** Failure mode (a): 'parent' means three things — (1) parent_chunk; (2) TreeNode.parent_ids (summary_tree.py:95) which are MEMBER parent-chunk ids, NOT tree-parents (tree children live in child_node_ids); (3) qdrant passthrough_parent_ids (parent-chunk ids again). A reader assuming parent_ids = tree ancestry will invert the hierarchy. **Evidence:** summary_tree.py:95-96: parent_ids '# L1 members (rollups)' vs child_node_ids '# tree children' — same row, both 'parent/child' words, orthogonal meanings.
- **hierarchy & headers — passthrough:** Failure mode (a): 'passthrough' elsewhere in the codebase means OpenAI-compatible provider passthrough (chat_orchestrator.py:6642, provider_presets.py:18, ghost_b.py:3974) and schema_lens id passthrough (ghost_b_local.py:322) — grep for 'passthrough' returns two unrelated subsystems. **Evidence:** backfill script docstring names the identity explicitly: 'singleton-section passthrough contract' (scripts/backfill_passthrough_sections.py:4).
- **hierarchy & headers — RAPTOR tree:** Failure mode (b), explicit: the built tree is NOT RAPTOR — RAPTOR_RAG_CRITIQUE_AND_ROADMAP.md:39 calls it 'closer to a doc-summary index + auto-merging than to RAPTOR' (no clustering, doc-gated). Meanwhile P3.1 plans a real clustering-based 'thematic RAPTOR layer' (checklist:1094,1124) as future work. Two different concepts share the name RAPTOR: the existing heading-grouped summary_tree (misnomer) and the to-be-built thematic layer. A grep-driven implementer could 'extend RAPTOR' in either place. **Evidence:** checklist:1124 'Only after success may the architecture claim a thematic RAPTOR layer' — the ledger itself acknowledges the current tree does not earn the name.
- **hierarchy & headers — singleton:** Failure mode (a), strong: 'Singleton' appears ~20x in backend meaning service-instance singletons (auth.py:294, llm.py:1237, tool_registry.py:215, facet_tagger.py:42...). The P0.2 open decision 'future singleton sections physically omitted or retained as aliases' (checklist:388-390) is PLAN-ONLY — no omit/alias branch exists in summary_tree.py; today every one-child section is retained and stamped with passthrough fields. **Evidence:** grep -rn singleton backend/services: zero tree-related hits; the concept lives entirely under the passthrough_* names.
- **hierarchy & headers — summary tree:** None internally — this is the one well-named concept; but note its Qdrant home is the generically-named 'schemas' collection kind shared with the entity lexicon (LEXICON_SCHEMA_KIND, qdrant_writer.py:1184), distinguished only by payload kind field. **Evidence:** Verified counts checklist:86 — 21,432 sections / 23,394 rollups; 20,942 one-child sections drove the passthrough work.
- **latent/underlying concepts — central_subjects:** (b) duplicate-implementation guard: central_subjects deliberately EXCLUDES latent_concepts — those go to the separate candidate_latent_subjects field because they are LLM-generated at ingest (card_builder.py:57-63) and can corroborate but never solely qualify a direct shelf (shelf_engine.py:15-17). S5/S8 rebuild work that 'rolls latent into the card' must preserve this two-field separation, not merge latent into central_subjects. Also note the cross-store rename central_subjects->subjects in slim payloads. **Evidence:** Implementation matches checklist:855 verbatim ('central_subjects: lexicon/Ghost B entities and profile concepts'); 673 cards built 2026-07-13 but pair cards miss the latent family entirely (PLAN_CRITIQUE:103) — an input-coverage gap, not a schema gap.
- **latent/underlying concepts — concept contract:** (a) CONFIRMED one-word-two-meanings: 'concept contract' names the corpus_lexicon card contract in P2.1 (checklist:813+, PLAN_CRITIQUE:36) and the Ghost A summary capture contract in P2.2/T-HOOK-2 (checklist:196-200, test_summary_semantics_latent.py:1). They live at different pipeline stages (per-parent capture vs corpus-level materialization). Any task saying just 'the concept contract' is ambiguous and must be disambiguated before implementation. **Evidence:** PLAN_CRITIQUE:36 groups 18 open P2.1 items under 'concept contract' (all lexicon-side); checklist:198 uses 'the Ghost A summary contract — the same seam that carries latent_concepts' for the other referent.
- **latent/underlying concepts — doc profile concepts:** (b) duplicate-implementation risk vs tree concepts: doc_profile.concepts IS the L4 document node's derive_node_concepts output stored a second time on documents — same lineage, one implementation, two stores. S5's rollup step 'parent latent_concepts -> doc profile concepts' (PLAN_CRITIQUE:84) must extend THIS projection, not derive a parallel doc-level concept list. **Evidence:** summary_tree.py:887 'concepts': profile.concepts with profile = nodes[-1] (document node built at :524 from section concepts); consumed by tier0 embed and card_builder — three names, one derivation.
- **latent/underlying concepts — hidden concept:** No code symbol named 'hidden*' exists anywhere in backend. Do not create one. **Evidence:** Only textual occurrence: docs/LATENT_CONCEPT_PROMPT.md:15 'identify latent concepts, hidden mechanisms, aliases' — 'hidden' is rhetorical flavor for the same latent_concepts output, not a second artifact.
- **latent/underlying concepts — latent concept:** None beyond the latent_topics/latent_terms word-collision noted on the latent_concepts row. **Evidence:** docs/LATENT_CONCEPT_PROMPT.md and tests/test_summary_semantics_latent.py both use the prose and snake_case forms interchangeably for the same artifact.
- **latent/underlying concepts — latent_concepts:** (a) word-collision: 'latent' also names an UNRELATED graph-discovery family — DiscoverResponse.latent_topics (backend/models/schemas.py:997, graph/orchestrator.py:5368 'signals') and trace latent_terms (schemas.py:878, polymath_mcp/tools.py:813). Never reconcile these with latent_concepts. (b) dark data: latent_concepts[].aliases are parsed+clamped but read by NOTHING (PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:68,106) — S5 alias-registry must consume, not re-capture. **Evidence:** Capture is implemented end-to-end into Mongo; consumption exists only via card_builder into candidate_latent_subjects, which shelf_engine treats as corroborate-only (shelf_engine.py:15-17). Coverage is the real gap: only 639 v2 parents carry it, PoC pair has zero (PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:60,100).
- **latent/underlying concepts — parent-level keywords:** (a) the plan's singular 'keywords' hides that it is THREE distinct Ghost A fields with different semantics (proper nouns vs transferable mechanisms vs normalized concepts); (b) keep distinct from latent_concepts — key_terms must appear verbatim in the passage, latent_concepts may be inferred. **Evidence:** checklist:157-163 Audit Delta 'backfill from parent mechanisms/key_terms' maps 1:1 onto summary_tree.py CONCEPT_SOURCE_FIELDS, which derive_node_concepts unions (summary_tree.py:39-68).
- **latent/underlying concepts — semantic_profile:** (a) name-adjacency hazard with THREE existing symbols: parent_chunks.semantic_chunk_type (its own aggregation source, summary_semantics.py:26 SEMANTIC_CHUNK_TYPES; card_builder.py:356,463), metadata.semantic_facets (worker.py:2916 _metadata_with_facets), and semantic_split chunking. Grep confirms zero 'semantic_profile' occurrences in backend code — it exists only at checklist:836-840. **Evidence:** Nearest existing seams to build on: (1) source = parent_chunks.semantic_chunk_type distribution over a concept's supporting parents (already read by card_builder for risks_or_likely_misuse); (2) home = corpus_lexicon entry materialization in backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py (the P2.1 contract file); (3) validated mechanism IDs already flow via card_builder mechanisms_taught (card_builder.py:427-447).
- **latent/underlying concepts — tree concepts:** (a) bare field name 'concepts' means different scopes in three stores (summary_tree.concepts per node, doc_profile.concepts per doc, Qdrant doc_summary payload 'concepts') — always qualify with the collection; (b) duplicate-implementation guard: any new backfill/derivation MUST import derive_node_concepts, exactly as backfill_tree_concepts.py does. **Evidence:** checklist:159-163 ('populate summary_tree.concepts at construction — the field exists but is never passed', merged 2026-07-13, 28,402 nodes filled) matches the shipped code.
- **latent/underlying concepts — tree_concepts:** Naming trap: a reader grepping for a 'tree_concepts' field finds only the script; do not add a field with this name — the Mongo field is 'concepts' on the summary_tree collection. **Evidence:** grep -rn tree_concepts backend/ hits only the script filename/backup prefix (backfill_tree_concepts.py:31,275).
- **latent/underlying concepts — underlying concept:** Zero grep hits for 'underlying concept' in backend or the two plan docs — plan-conversation synonym only. **Evidence:** grep -rn 'underlying' over docs/RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md, docs/PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md, backend/: no concept-family hits.
- **librarian cards/shelves/seats — adjacent:** None. **Evidence:** shelf_engine.py:20-23; checklist:649-650
- **librarian cards/shelves/seats — bridge:** FAILURE MODE (b) — duplicate-implementation risk: P3.2 'bridge cards' (checklist:1141-1147, PLAN-ONLY) and 'transfer_edge' rows (checklist:319,1149-1155, PLAN-ONLY) both materialize the SAME evidence join _role_bridge computes at query time (shared principle/mechanism + different central subjects + two-sided evidence). If built without reusing shelf_engine.principle_bridge/_role_bridge criteria, the bridge notion will exist twice with drifting thresholds. - **readiness & enrichment states — metadata-quality (readiness):** No code symbol, no store field, no grep hit for metadata_quality anywhere in backend/. To-be-built. - **readiness & enrichment states — operational readiness:** Failure mode (b): 'operational readiness' has NO code symbol of its own — it is a plan-side rename of the existing corpus_readiness.v2 computation. - **readiness & enrichment states — publication gate:** Failure mode (a): the verb 'publish' already has a DIFFERENT code meaning in this family — corpus_lexicon.finalize_corpus_lexicon_index docstring 'reconcile ... then publish readiness' (corpus_lexicon.py:2966) means flipping lexicon_state to lexicon_ready, not card publication. A 'publication gate' grep will hit the lexicon path first. - **readiness & enrichment states — readiness (unqualified):** Failure mode (a) CONFIRMED, 5-way: 'readiness' names five distinct mechanisms. Any new plan work saying 'readiness' must pick a qualifier. **Evidence:** Five files, five meanings, all grep 'readiness': readiness.py:1 ('Computed corpus-readiness contract'), retrieval_readiness.py:1 ('Retrieval readiness guards'), health_service.py:239 (P1.8 tri-state), extraction_validation.py:1 (deploy-readiness probes), corpus_lexicon.py:2966 ('publish readiness' = flip lexicon_state).

- **readiness & enrichment states — strict ready (Definition Of Strict Ready):** Failure mode (b) HIGH RISK: no strict_ready symbol exists anywhere in backend (grep -rniE 'strict[_ -]read' backend/ = zero hits), yet ~80% of its definition is ALREADY computed by compute_corpus_readiness (queryable/summary/tree-sync/lexicon-projection/no-failed-jobs/denominator exclusions, readiness.py:1105+). - **readiness & enrichment states — temporal capability:** Two collisions. (a) 'temporal_support' already exists in models/schemas.py meaning 'this graph packet's evidence has dates' — do not reuse that name for the capability tier. - **retrieval mechanics — answerability:** One concept, two INTENTIONAL mirror implementations — chat refusal gate vs retriever repair sufficiency (documented at ranking_policy.py:1080-1087: 'Do not loosen criticality here'). Secondary risk (b): three sufficiency-producer branches in retriever/__init__.py:3159-3200 each rebuild the same payload shape by hand; the 'source' field is the only discriminator. **Evidence:** P0.4 checklist:439-462 explicitly renamed/split lane coverage from answerability; memory + code NOTE confirm the two-gate architecture is deliberate.
- **retrieval mechanics — evidence atom:** FAILURE MODE (b), mild: 'concept:*' atoms are minted by two independent generators — query_semantics.concept_groups (concept:{group.key}) and retriever lane-id synthesis (concept:{lane_id}, retriever/__init__.py:3144). - **retrieval mechanics — hydration:** Code hydration = chunk-text enrichment, and it EXISTS. Must stay ONE enum (evergreen\|slowly_evolving\|versioned\|event\|ephemeral\|unknown) fed by ONE classifier, or per-carrier drift becomes failure mode (a). **Evidence:** grep -rn temporal_class backend/ = 0 hits; PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md:67 confirms 'zero rows anywhere carry temporal_class'. T-HOOK-2 (CHECKLIST:195-198) targets the Ghost A summary contract. Nearest existing seam: backend/services/ghost_a.py:239-260 SummaryResult dataclass — latent_concepts sits at ghost_a.py:260 and is hydrated at ghost_a.py:649; temporal_class is specified to ride that exact seam. Backfill for existing rows = deterministic classifier (T-MAIN Phase 3), never paid regen.
- **temporal metadata — temporal_unavailable:** Lands on the SAME seam as the S6 readiness split (operational vs metadata-quality vs temporal capability); the enum must live beside — not inside — fully_enriched, because fully_enriched is a pipeline-stage label (backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py:112 FULLY_ENRICHED_STAGES) that must stop implying librarian-grade or temporal-grade metadata (CHECKLIST:169-171). **Evidence:** grep -rn temporal_unavailable backend/ = 0 hits. Nearest existing seam: backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py — build_corpus_readiness_record (:309), build_corpus_readiness_snapshot (:330), compute_corpus_readiness (:1105); CHECKLIST:210 says 'same seam as the operational-vs-metadata-quality readiness split adopted in Audit Delta 2'.
- **temporal metadata — time_expressions:** FAILURE MODE (a): the same name time_expressions is ALSO planned as a NEW Ghost A summary-contract field (T-HOOK-2, CHECKLIST:195-198) — summary-level time expressions, a different seam from the built chunk-level extraction capture. When S1 builds it, either persist the summary field under a distinct name or document scope explicitly: chunk-level = temporal_captures (built), summary-level = T-HOOK-2 (unbuilt). **Evidence:** runpod_flash_extraction.py:411-420 comment states the rename explicitly ('extraction rows persist them as temporal_captures + temporal_capture_version'). One concept, deliberate name drift at the wire boundary.

TERMINOLOGY (registry_retrieval 'tier'): 'tier' is 4-way overloaded — RetrievalTier (this invariant), SourceChunk.source_tier, Tier-0 routing (NOT a RetrievalTier), and tier_chunker Tiers A/B/C. 'Fast' is the checklist alias for qdrant_only (eval_metrics.py:214 is authoritative). | Per-change: three EVAL/probe artifacts (one per tier) with requested_tier==effective_tier asserted; graph tier not silently collapsed. |

The invariant is enforceable today on the built path. | Retrieval trace for a probe query shows routing -> document -> tree descent -> parent/child order with no un-routed child fetches. |

Tests must target the lane sense specifically. | Asserting test: original lane present in every plan; fusion output non-empty and stable when expansion lanes are ablated. |
