### P1 Latency And Resource Isolation (frozen lines 697-812, chunk P1L)

| ID | Claim (verbatim) | Kind / status | Disposition | Auditable action | Who | Where | When | Why | Feasibility | Required receipt |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P1L:L701 | Gather all plan embedding texts before vocabulary/tree/funnel fan-out. | work (open) | build | Build gather-before-fanout in the retriever: collect every plan lane text needing an embedding (planner lanes, vocabulary resolution queries, summary_tree descent queries, Funnel A breadth) into one embed batch before any Qdrant fan-out; verify with a trace probe asserting exactly one embedder call batch per query where lanes share texts. | junior-agent (retriever code change + trace probe) | backend/services/retriever/ (query_plan.py fan-out seam, vocabulary.py, tree descent, funnel path); embedder_mlx :8082 client | S5 | One embed round-trip instead of N serial sidecar calls directly cuts vocabulary/tree p50 on the shared Metal GPU. | feasible — Unslotted in the S0-S14 table but named a P2.2 prerequisite via P1.7 (PLAN_CRITIQUE S10 row: 'P1.7 batching+lexical-only baseline (S0/S5)'), so it must close in the S5 window before S10. Terminology: 'vocabulary' here = the query-time vocabulary RESOLUTION stage, not the corpus_lexicon store (registry_alias 'vocabulary' OVERLAPPING — call the store corpus_lexicon); 'tree' = summary_tree (registry_hierarchy canonical); 'funnel' = Funnel A parent-level summary breadth, not Tier-0 doc_summaries (registry_hierarchy 'doc summary' collision). | Retrieval trace on a multi-lane query showing a single batched embed call covering plan+vocabulary+tree texts (embed_calls counter before/after), plus vocabulary p50 delta vs the pre-change latency probe; asserting test exit 0. |
| P1L:L702 | Reuse vectors across planner, vocabulary, tree, and retrieval lanes. | work (open) | build | Implement per-request vector reuse: key embedded texts by normalized text within one retrieval request and pass the same vector object to planner, vocabulary resolution, summary_tree descent, and recall-lane search instead of re-embedding; assert via a counter test that identical lane texts embed exactly once per request. | junior-agent (retriever code change + asserting test) | backend/services/retriever/ (shared embed cache per request context); embedder_mlx :8082 call sites | S5 | Duplicate embeddings of the same query text are pure wasted GPU time on the contended Metal device. | feasible — Companion to L701; same S5 window and same P2.2-prereq pressure (PLAN_CRITIQUE S10). Registry note applies: bare 'planner' is ambiguous — deterministic QueryPlanV2 (retriever/query_plan.py) vs LLM grounded_planner are different components (registry_retrieval 'grounded planner'); this item concerns the deterministic planner's lane texts. Scope reuse per-request only; cross-request reuse is L706's cache and must not be duplicated here (Standing Rule 2 discipline). | Asserting test: one retrieval request with N lanes sharing a text embeds it once (embed-call counter == unique texts); probe exit 0 in the deployed backend container. |

| P1L:L705 | Keep exact alias payload lookup logically separate from dense requests. | work (open) | build | While implementing L703/L761 batching, keep the exact alias payload lookup (scroll/filter on aliases_normalized in the corpus_{cid8}_schemas lexicon points) as a separate call path from approximate dense query_batch_points; add an asserting test that exact-alias results are byte-identical pre/post batching. | junior-agent (design constraint enforced in the L703 change) | backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py (_TRUSTED_EXACT_ALIAS_METHODS path); Qdrant corpus_{cid8}_schemas payload aliases_normalized | S5 | Exact alias hits are deterministic identity evidence; folding them into ANN search would make exact matches probabilistic and silently lossy. | feasible — This is a correctness constraint on L703, not independent work — schedule as one change. Terminology: 'alias' is 5-way overloaded (registry_alias 'aliases' OVERLAPPING); here it means the lexicon alias (identity evidence) payload fields (aliases/aliases_normalized on schemas points), the only retrieval-live meaning — not curated CONCEPT_ALIASES, latent aliases, or graph alias maps. | Asserting test in the batching PR: exact-alias resolution results identical before/after batching (set diff empty), and code path shows separate scroll vs query_batch_points calls; exit 0. |

| P1L:L758 | Replace with_payload=True on hot candidate searches with explicit field selectors, preserving every field consumed by ranking, hydration, citations, and diagno… | work (open) | build | Inventory every consumer of hot-path candidate payloads (ranking features, hydration keys, citation fields, diagnostics) into an explicit field list; replace with_payload=True with that selector; assert zero field-miss via a differential test (full-payload run vs selector run must produce identical ranked+cited output). | junior-agent (consumer audit + retriever change + differential test) | backend retriever hot candidate search calls; ranking/hydration/citation consumers; Qdrant child collections | S5 | Halves hot-path transfer (292->148 KB per L748) and p50 payload cost (14->6ms band) without touching recall. | risky — Mechanically proven (L748 measured the selector working), but the risk is the completeness audit: a missed diagnostics or citation field fails SILENTLY (evidence renders without provenance) — the exact silent-fallback class the standing discipline forbids. | P1L:L765 | Audit the 29 payload indexes repeated on each route collection; retain hot filter fields in RAM and remove only indexes proven unused by retrieval, graph, read… | work (open) | build | For each of the 29 per-collection payload indexes, produce a proof-of-use table across ALL five consumer path families (retrieval, graph, readiness, deletion, repair) by grepping filter construction; propose removals only for indexes with zero hits in all five; owner reviews the removal list; execute removals in the same S5/S7 payload pass and record memory reclaimed. | junior-agent (proof-of-use audit) + owner (removal list approval) | Qdrant payload indexes on every route collection; backend filter-construction sites across retriever/graph/readiness/deletion/repair | S5 | Index RAM is the recoverable headroom on an 83%-full node, but a wrongly-dropped index turns a repair path into a full scan. | risky — The audit is cheap; the removal is the risk — deletion and repair paths run rarely, so 'proven unused by retrieval' is NOT proof of unused (the item's own five-path list is the right bar). The failure mode is silent slowness in an emergency path, discovered during an incident. Hence owner sign-off on the removal list and Standing Rule 4 co-scheduling with the S5/S7 payload rewrite so collections are touched once. Keep 'retain hot filter fields in RAM' as the default; removal is the exception. | Proof-of-use matrix (29 indexes x 5 path families with grep evidence), owner-approved removal list, and post-removal memory delta + one exercised deletion/repair operation timed within norms. |
| P1L:L768 | Define interactive and bulk-ingest Qdrant profiles. Under concurrent writes, benchmark smaller upsert batches and max_optimization_threads=1 before changing de… | work (open) | build | Define two named Qdrant client/config profiles (interactive vs bulk-ingest); then run a concurrent-write benchmark on a clone/canary collection comparing current defaults vs smaller upsert batches and max_optimization_threads=1, measuring query p95 during writes; adopt changes only if the benchmark wins. | script (benchmark harness) + junior-agent; owner only if defaults change system-wide | Qdrant client config (upsert batch size), collection optimizer config; ingest-worker write path (polymath_v33-ingest-worker-1) | S4 | The S4 paid backfill is the first sustained concurrent-write load; unbenchmarked defaults risk interactive p95 blowouts during it. | feasible — The item is correctly benchmark-gated ('before changing defaults') — matching the hypothesis-promotion rule (numeric targets set in advance). Schedule the benchmark BEFORE S4's pair backfill so the bulk profile exists when the heavy write pass runs; UGO_CORPUS canary is the designated guinea pig. Note writes originate from the ingest-worker container, not the backend (offline-ingest overlay) — profile plumbing must reach that container's client. | Benchmark table: query p50/p95 during concurrent writes under {default, small-batch, small-batch+opt_threads=1}; adopted profile named in config with the winning numbers; interactive p95 during S4 within the L746-derived target. |
| P1L:L771 | Do not enable prevent_unoptimized until every write explicitly uses the intended wait semantics and readiness proves deferred points are searchable. | invariant (open) | standing-invariant | Keep prevent_unoptimized OFF (standing gate). Before any future enable: audit every upsert/delete call for explicit wait semantics and add a readiness check proving deferred (unoptimized-segment) points are returned by search; only then bring an enable proposal to the owner. | owner (any future enable decision); junior-agent (precondition audit if proposed) | Qdrant collection config; every writer call site (qdrant_writer.py, ingest-worker, backfill scripts) wait= parameters; readiness probes | CONT | Enabling it prematurely turns ingest-time optimizer lag into user-visible write failures or invisible unsearchable points. | feasible — Correctly framed as a do-not-until invariant; nothing in S0-S14 requires prevent_unoptimized, so the default disposition is 'never, unless a concrete failure motivates it'. The precondition (explicit wait semantics on every write) is auditable by grep, but the second precondition (readiness proves deferred points searchable) has no current instrument — it would need a new probe. Treat as blocked-by-design until an owner-level need appears. | Standing: config dump showing prevent_unoptimized disabled. If ever proposed: wait-semantics audit table + deferred-point searchability probe green, plus owner approval note. |
| P1L:L773 | Keep current single-shard/single-replica topology on this one-node Mac; replicas and extra shards are not a free local speedup. | invariant (open) | standing-invariant | Keep single-shard/single-replica on the one-node Mac (standing gate): assert in the collection-creation code/config that shard_number=1, replication_factor=1, and add a census check to the readiness/census probes flagging any collection that deviates. | backend service (collection creation defaults) + census probe | qdrant_writer.py collection creation params; census/readiness probes | CONT | Extra shards/replicas on one node add merge and memory overhead with zero availability gain. | feasible — Physics-sound invariant for a single-node deployment (RAM at 82.9% makes replicas actively harmful). No plan item proposes sharding; P3.3 consolidation (S13) is the only structural change on the horizon and it reduces, not increases, collection count. Cheap to enforce mechanically in the census probe rather than as tribal knowledge. | Census probe output: all collections shard_number=1, replication_factor=1; creation-path default asserted by test. |
| P1L:L775 | Pin the Qdrant image version after the performance contract is validated; do not deploy latest as the production data-store contract. | work (open) | build | After the P1.9 performance targets are validated (L780-L784 green), pin the exact Qdrant image digest (qdrant:1.18.0@sha256:...) in docker-compose and record the pin + validation date in the ledger; forbid 'latest' in any compose file via a CI/grep check. | junior-agent (compose pin + grep guard); owner informed of the pinned version | docker-compose.yml (+ override/overlay files — all three compose files per deploy topology); ledger baseline header | S14 | An unpinned data-store image can silently change performance and on-disk format under the validated contract. | feasible — Trivially feasible; the only judgment is timing — the item itself says pin AFTER the performance contract is validated, which lands at the S14 regression/deploy gate (baselines re-captured there). Interim risk: L744's baseline is against 1.18.0 while the tag may float; if any unexplained perf shift appears before S14, pin immediately at 1.18.0 rather than waiting. Deploy gotcha from memory: recreation must merge ALL compose files (override drops kill sidecars silently) — the pin edit must go in the file actually used by the deploy command. | Compose diff showing digest-pinned Qdrant image + grep proving no ':latest' remains + a restart proving the pinned image serves (telemetry version matches pin). |
| P1L:L780 | Qdrant remains below its memory warning gate during normal retrieval. | acceptance (open) | standing-invariant | Wire/verify a memory gate check: readiness or monitoring asserts Qdrant memory below its warning threshold during a standard retrieval load probe; run it after every P1.9 change and during S4/S11 bulk passes. | script (resource probe in latency harness); backend readiness gate | Qdrant container memory stats; readiness memory warning gate config | CONT | At 82.9% baseline, any regression (new indexes, more collections) tips into swap/OOM territory where all latency numbers die. | feasible — Acceptance framed as a continuous condition, so treat as a standing gate, not a one-shot box. It is TIGHT today: 6.63/8 GiB (L750) leaves <1.4 GiB headroom, and L763 adds indexes while S4/S5 add points — the box likely only stays green if L765/L751 reclamation lands in the same window. Define the numeric warning threshold explicitly (it must exist as config, not vibes) before calling this checked. | Probe output during normal retrieval + during a bulk pass showing memory below the configured warning threshold, with the threshold value named; attached to each P1.9 change receipt. |
| P1L:L781 | Candidate payload bytes fall without losing ranking or citation fields. | acceptance (open) | build | Close via L758's differential test plus byte accounting: candidate payload bytes per query drop (target ~50% per L748) while ranked output, citations, and diagnostics fields are byte-identical to the full-payload control. | script (differential probe from L758) | hot candidate search path; trace result-bytes field (from L756) | S5 | This is the acceptance that L758 bought bytes without silently losing ranking/citation inputs. | feasible — Pure consequence of L758 + L756 instrumentation; no independent risk. The 'without losing fields' half is the same differential test demanded there — one artifact satisfies both lines. Numeric target is pre-set by the L748 measurement (292->148 KB band), matching the hypothesis-promotion discipline. | Per-query bytes before/after (from trace) + identical ranked/cited output diff; both attached to the L758 receipt. |
| P1L:L782 | Query latency during a bounded ingest stays within the measured target and ingestion remains durable/queryable. | acceptance (open) | build | During a bounded ingest (canary or S4 tranche), run the three-tier latency probe concurrently and assert query latency stays within the L746-derived target while the ingest completes durably and its points are searchable afterward. | script (concurrent probe: probe_tier_latency.py + bounded ingest driver) | deployed backend + ingest-worker container + Qdrant; UGO_CORPUS canary first | S4 | The S4 paid pass is exactly this scenario at scale; this acceptance is its preflight canary. | feasible — Well-posed only after 'measured target' is pinned: L746 gives Qdrant-side ms targets but end-to-end query latency needs the agreed p95 from L796's 'agreed p95' — set the number BEFORE the run (preflight-canary discipline from memory). Depends on L768's profiles if the default config fails the probe. Durability half is cheap: post-ingest count + sample search of newly-written points. | Concurrent-run report: query p50/p95 during ingest vs target, ingest completion (counts match), and post-ingest searchability of new points; exit 0. |
| P1L:L784 | End-to-end latency reporting distinguishes Qdrant time from embedding, planning, vocabulary, reranking, Mongo hydration, Neo4j, and synthesis. | acceptance (open) | build | Extend the retrieval trace/latency report to attribute end-to-end time across: Qdrant, embedding, planning, vocabulary, reranking, Mongo hydration, Neo4j, synthesis — and assert the parts sum to within a tolerance of the whole on live queries. | junior-agent (trace schema + report change, building on L756) | backend trace/diagnostics schema; probe_tier_latency.py report; chat trace metadata | S5 | Stage attribution is what turns 'Fast is 39.8s degraded' from a mystery into a work item. | feasible — Superset of L756 (which covers the Qdrant stages); most non-Qdrant stages already have partial timing in traces — the work is normalizing to one report with a sum-check so unattributed time is visible instead of hidden. Must land before S8's A/B for the same reason as L756. The sum-vs-total tolerance check is the anti-silent-gap device (silent-fallback accounting rule). | One live query report listing all eight stage timings + unattributed remainder < tolerance; three-tier probe output includes the breakdown; exit 0. |
| P1L:L786 | Verify ingestion/backfill calls consistently use lower-priority classes. | acceptance (open) | build | Verify by grep + live trace that every ingestion/backfill embed and rerank call declares a lower-priority (backfill/bulk) class: enumerate caller sites in ingest-worker and backfill scripts, then run one backfill tranche and read the sidecar's admission log/stats for class labels. | junior-agent (call-site audit + canary tranche probe) | ingest-worker container call sites; backfill scripts (e.g. summary_backfill.py); embedder_mlx :8082 admission stats | S4 | One mislabeled bulk caller at interactive class defeats the whole P1.8 isolation design during the paid pass. | feasible — Preflight for S4 (preflight-canary discipline). The S0 warmup receipt incidentally proves the backfill class is honored server-side ('warmup ... at backfill class'); what remains is caller coverage — including the OFFLINE ingest-worker container's code path, which is a different container from the backend (memory: offline-ingest overlay) and is easy to miss in a backend-only grep. | Caller audit table (site x declared class) with zero interactive-class bulk callers + one tranche's admission stats showing all bulk requests at backfill class; restamp box. |
| P1L:L787 | Add or verify reranker-side priority admission. | acceptance (open) | build | Audit the deployed :8081 reranker for admission/priority classes equivalent to the embedder's; if absent, port the embedder_mlx admission-gate pattern (priority/FIFO classes + queue caps) to the reranker sidecar and verify with a two-class contention probe. | junior-agent (sidecar audit + port + probe) | :8081 torch fp16 reranker sidecar (plist-managed, ~/PolymathRuntime copy); rollback reranker_mlx | S5 | Backfill-driven rerank load can starve interactive reranking exactly as embedding load did before P1.8. | feasible — 'Add or verify' — verification first: no receipt exists either way. Registry warning applies (registry_leftover 'reranker readiness'): the deployed binary is the torch fp16 cross-encoder, reranker_mlx is rollback — audit the binary actually serving :8081, and remember sidecars deploy from the ~/PolymathRuntime COPY (repo edits need re-install). Contention design note from memory: rerank and embed share one Metal GPU, and support-pass reranking is already gated off (RERANK_EVIDENCE_SUPPORT) — admission classes complement, not replace, that rule. Also mind the reranker death-spiral rule: any queueing added must never split-retry timed-out requests. | Either an audit receipt showing existing class support on :8081 (config + stats) or the ported gate with a probe: interactive rerank admitted ahead of a queued bulk batch; suite exit 0. |
| P1L:L788 | Measure whether keepalive provides value after startup warmup. | acceptance (open) | build | Measure keepalive value post-warmup: after S0's warmup (L732), compare first-request latency after 10/30/60-minute idle windows with and without a periodic keepalive ping on embedder (:8082) and reranker (:8081); adopt keepalive only if idle decay is demonstrated. | script (idle-decay timing probe) | embedder_mlx :8082, reranker :8081; optional keepalive timer (launchd or backend task) | S5 | Warmup fixed cold start; keepalive is only worth its GPU pings if MPS/Metal actually decays warm state during idle. | feasible — Cheap, honest experiment with a built-in null hypothesis — the correct default is NO keepalive unless decay is measured (avoid adding standing GPU load to a contended Metal device per the GPU-contention rule). Registry disambiguation: 'keepalive after startup warmup' is a distinct concern from P1.8 inference warmup and from Qdrant compile cost (registry_leftover 'inference warmup' note) — do not restamp P1.8 boxes off this measurement. | Idle-decay table: first-call latency vs idle duration, keepalive on/off, both sidecars; decision line (adopt/skip) with numbers; if adopted, the keepalive mechanism + its measured cost. |

(merged + deployed both accounts + live-verified 2026-07-13 ...) | acceptance (open) | done-verified | Accept receipt (verified 2026-07-13): separate ingestion embed capacity EXISTS and is validated — RunPod embed lane merged + deployed on both accounts (k695blmk52oscm / hlp9h3o4zd0v4d), dim 1024, dual-account routing live-verified. Remaining closure: record whether local contention persists post-P1.8 (the item's own trigger condition) and keep use inside the L790 promotion gate. | junior-agent (receipt acceptance + post-P1.8 contention measurement); owner (any expansion of use) | runpod_flash_embedder/ lane; RunPod endpoints on both accounts; local embedder_mlx :8082 contention stats | LOG | Off-box embed capacity is the escape valve if backfill still contends with interactive embedding after admission classes. | already-done — The evaluation the box asks for is done and receipted in the ledger itself (live-verified 2026-07-13). Registry: 'RunPod' is two lanes (extraction vs embed) with different contracts (registry_extraction 'RunPod' OVERLAPPING) — this receipt covers the EMBED lane only. | Ledger receipt line (both account IDs, dim 1024, dual routing) + an S4-window contention reading that either justifies bulk use of the RunPod lane or shows local classes suffice. |

Re-check the 0.98 cosine receipt if either side's model/quantization changes. | owner (gate release decision after A/B); script (cosine parity re-probe on model change) | runpod_flash_embedder lane vs local embedder_mlx (Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B-mxfp8); every Qdrant collection's embedding-source provenance | CONT | 0.98 cosine between local-mxfp8 and remote-fp is close but NOT identity — mixing sources in one collection would skew neighbor geometry unpredictably. | feasible — Sound, receipted gate (2026-07-13, cosine 0.98, dim 1024). The held-out recall A/B that could relax the gate naturally lands with the S8/S10 eval harness; until then whole-corpus experiments must be all-remote or all-local per collection. | Standing: per-collection single embed source (all-local or all-remote) verifiable by census; gate release requires a held-out recall A/B report (local vs remote vs mixed) with no recall loss, owner-signed. |

| P1L:L796 | Backfill cannot force interactive embedding/reranking beyond agreed p95. | acceptance (open) | build | Set the agreed interactive p95 number in advance (from the L746/L744 baseline + current three-tier probe), then run a sustained backfill while firing interactive queries; assert interactive embed+rerank p95 stays under the agreed bound thanks to admission classes. | script (contention probe harness); owner sets/accepts the p95 number | embedder_mlx :8082 + reranker :8081 admission gates; backfill driver (ingest-worker); three-tier probe | S4 | This is the load-bearing guarantee of the isolation program: paid backfills cannot degrade the user's chat. | feasible — Mechanism is in place (L730 classes, L786 caller audit pending); what is missing is the 'agreed p95' — no numeric target exists yet in the ledger, and per the hypothesis-promotion rule it must be fixed BEFORE the probe run, not read off the result. Natural slot: preflight + during the S4 paid pass, sharing harness with L782/L786. Reranker-side coverage depends on L787 landing first (embedder-only classes leave a rerank hole in this guarantee). | Named p95 target recorded first; then contention-run report showing interactive p95 <= target while backfill sustains; both attached to the S4 preflight. |
| P1L:L797 | Cold-start and concurrent-query tests no longer degrade unexpectedly. | acceptance (open) | build | Add cold-start and concurrent-query scenarios to the standing latency harness (restart-then-query; N parallel queries) and run them before/after each P1 change; assert no unexplained degradation vs the recorded baseline band. | script (extend probe_tier_latency.py or sibling harness) | deployed backend + sidecars; docs/baselines latency artifacts | S8 | Regression detection is what makes every earlier P1 receipt durable instead of a one-day snapshot. | feasible — 'No longer degrade unexpectedly' is only falsifiable against a recorded expectation — the harness must persist a baseline band (p50/p95 + tolerance) per scenario. Cold-start scenario is cheap post-L794; concurrent-query needs care on the shared Metal GPU (parallel rerank is a known pathology per the GPU-contention rule — the harness should measure, not mask, that). Slot at S8 where the A/B machinery runs anyway; earlier ad-hoc runs welcome. | Harness output: cold-start and concurrent scenarios with recorded baseline bands and a green run post-change; wired into the S8 A/B and S14 regression matrix. |
| P1L:L801 | Measure reranker latency by candidate count and token length. | work (open) | build | Instrument and sweep: measure deployed :8081 rerank latency across a grid of candidate counts (e.g. 10/25/50/85) x passage token lengths, producing a cost model table that L803's budget selection will consume. | script (rerank latency sweep against :8081) | :8081 torch fp16 cross-encoder sidecar; probe artifact in docs/baselines/ | S5 | Adaptive budgets need a real cost curve; without it 'adaptive' is guessing. | feasible — Pure measurement, no risk. Section-id note: this 'Make Reranking Adaptive' section is the SECOND section labeled P1.9 (critique C4 flags the duplicate id) — receipts should cite 'P1.9-adaptive' to stay unambiguous. Run the sweep serially (never parallel rerank batches on the shared Metal GPU per the contention rule) and on the true fp16 cross-encoder now serving :8081, not the rollback MLX backend. | Latency grid artifact (candidates x token-length -> p50/p95) with sidecar backend identity recorded; filed under docs/baselines with date. |
| P1L:L802 | Generate query-relevant excerpts before cross-encoder scoring. | work (open) | build | Prototype query-relevant excerpting (window selection around match spans) feeding the cross-encoder shorter passages; gate adoption on a held-out A/B showing latency gain with zero recall/nDCG loss vs full-passage scoring. | junior-agent (excerpt generator + A/B harness) | backend rerank preparation path (candidate text assembly before :8081 call); held-out eval set | S9 | CE cost scales with token length; excerpts attack the second axis of L801's cost model. | risky — Changes the CE's INPUT — under the production-redesign direction the cross-encoder is the sole scoring authority, so feeding it excerpts can silently change ranking semantics; evidence relevant outside the excerpt window is scored as absent. This is exactly the class needing the P1.1 held-out gate (hypothesis-promotion rule: numeric recall/nDCG targets fixed in advance). Do not ship as a default without the A/B; consider shape-conditional use (long list/synthesis passages) per L805. | Held-out A/B report: rerank latency delta + recall/nDCG deltas (target: latency down, quality delta >= 0) vs full-passage control; owner-visible adopt/skip decision line. |
| P1L:L803 | Select candidate budget by answer shape, score cliff, and retrieval uncertainty. | work (open) | build | Implement candidate-budget selection using (a) answer shape (P1.6 shape router), (b) score-cliff detection on retrieval scores, (c) retrieval-uncertainty signal; consume L801's cost table to pick the budget; verify with per-shape before/after latency and held-out quality. | junior-agent (rerank budget policy in retriever) | backend rerank dispatch (candidate count selection before :8081); consumes P1.6 answer-shape routing + L801 cost model | S9 | Most queries need far fewer than max candidates reranked; cliffs and shape tell us which. | feasible — Correctly sequenced only at/after S9: 'answer shape' has no implementation yet (P1.6 all boxes open, PLAN_CRITIQUE sec.1) — building shape-conditional budgets before shape routing exists would hardcode a stub. Score-cliff machinery has precedent in P1.6's 'dynamic breadth/K based on score cliffs' — implement ONCE and share, or the two items become duplicate cliff detectors (registry failure-mode (b) pattern). Held-out gate mandatory as with L802. | Policy code + per-shape budget table; held-out A/B: latency down materially, recall/nDCG flat; trace shows chosen budget + trigger (shape/cliff/uncertainty) per query. |
| P1L:L805 | Preserve broader pools for cross-domain and list questions. | work (open) | build | Encode a floor rule in the L803 policy: cross-domain and list/enumeration shapes keep expanded candidate pools (no aggressive budget cuts); add held-out cases of both shapes to the A/B so the floor is asserted, not assumed. | junior-agent (policy floor + eval cases) | rerank budget policy (with L803); held-out eval set list/cross-domain rows | S9 | Budget cuts hurt exactly the query shapes that need breadth — this floor protects them by construction. | feasible — A constraint on L803 rather than separate work — same change, same A/B. It also aligns with the three-tier regression matrix rows (list, cross-domain, cross-corpus are named row shapes) so the S14 matrix will re-assert it. Shape detection dependency is the same P1.6 prerequisite as L803. | Policy test: list + cross-domain queries receive >= floor candidate budgets; held-out list/cross-domain rows show no recall loss under the adaptive policy. |
| P1L:L806 | Evaluate cascade or late-interaction alternatives before model changes. | work (open) | build | Before any reranker MODEL change (P3.5 territory), run a bounded evaluation of (a) cascade reranking (cheap scorer prunes, CE scores survivors) and (b) late-interaction (ColBERT-style) alternatives on the held-out set, comparing latency/quality against the adaptive-budget CE from L803. | junior-agent (experiment harness); owner (any model adoption decision) | experiment branch; :8081 CE as control; held-out eval set | S13 | Cheaper architectures may beat budget-tuning, but only measurement — not fashion — should replace the CE. | feasible — Explicitly an ordering rule ('before model changes') — it gates P3.5 (reranker serving experiment, S13) and inherits Experiment status: no production adoption without before/after evaluation. Under the production-redesign decision the CE is the sole scoring authority, so any cascade's cheap first stage must be shown not to prune CE-winners (measure first-stage recall@survivors). Do this only after L801-L805 land, since the adaptive CE is the honest baseline to beat. | Experiment report: cascade and late-interaction vs adaptive-CE control on held-out (latency, recall/nDCG, first-stage prune loss); explicit adopt/reject line signed by owner. |
| P1L:L810 | Rerank latency improves materially with no held-out recall/nDCG loss. | acceptance (open) | build | Acceptance A/B for the adaptive-rerank train (L801-L805): rerank stage latency improves materially (set the numeric bar from the L801 cost table in advance) with held-out recall/nDCG deltas >= 0 across shapes. | script (held-out A/B harness) | rerank stage traces + held-out eval artifacts (EVAL_*.json) | S9 | The single gate that converts the adaptive-rerank hypothesis into a promoted behavior. | feasible — Depends on P1.1 held-out set being complete with the contamination check (P1.1 has 3 open boxes incl. the automated contamination eval-run check, which the plan lands at S10 — run at S9 with at least the manual contamination discipline). 'Materially' must be pre-quantified (hypothesis-promotion rule) — propose: p50 rerank stage time down >= 30% on non-list shapes, from the L801 curve. | A/B report: rerank p50/p95 before/after vs the pre-registered bar + recall/nDCG per shape (no loss); attached to the ledger line with date. |
| P1L:L811 | Fast is meaningfully faster than Hybrid while remaining useful. | acceptance (open) | build | Measure tier separation post-changes: run the three-tier latency probe and assert qdrant_only (Fast) p50 is materially below qdrant_mongo (Hybrid) while Fast's held-out answer quality stays above its usefulness floor. | script (probe_tier_latency.py + held-out Fast-tier rows) | three retrieval tiers of the deployed backend; probe + eval artifacts | S9 | If Fast is not actually fast, the tier contract is marketing — users pay Hybrid cost either way. | feasible — Terminology (registry_leftover/registry_retrieval): 'Fast' = RetrievalTier.qdrant_only, 'Hybrid' = RetrievalTier.qdrant_mongo — and 'hybrid' ALSO names dense+sparse vector search inside Qdrant, an orthogonal mechanism; keep receipts in tier enum names. Known bad datum: ledger header records 'Fast 39.8s degraded' — this box is currently FAILING on its face, and the fix is the whole P1 train (cache L706, warmup L732, batching L703, adaptive rerank) rather than a single item; assert it after S9. 'Remaining useful' needs a defined floor (held-out Fast-tier pass rate) set in advance. | Three-tier probe: qdrant_only p50/p95 materially below qdrant_mongo (pre-registered ratio) + Fast-tier held-out pass rate >= floor; filed with tier enum names and date. |

Verify per merge via Mongo collection listing + grep for new concept/lexicon collection names outside corpus_lexicon. | junior-agent (merge-review gate) | backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py; Mongo corpus_lexicon; Qdrant entity_lexicon points | CONT | One authoritative store keeps concept evidence joinable, rebuildable, and epoch-invalidatable for retrieval. | feasible — corpus_lexicon.v3 already IS the single store (LEXICON_SCHEMA_VERSION at corpus_lexicon.py:37). 'What is X?' needs only canonical name and can ship immediately. Provenance class of these points = derived (registry: templates are derived, NOT generated) — mislabeling them generated would wrongly subject them to Phase-4 gating; mislabeling extracted would overstate evidence. | Sample of 20 templated points on mark: each carries template version + feeding field + span/source IDs that resolve; zero points whose text is not a deterministic function of indexed fields. |

Design note: decide whether epoch is IN the ID (supersede-by-new-id + delete-old) or only in payload (overwrite-in-place); the lexicon precedent uses stable IDs + deferred stale deletion — follow it. | Probe: rebuild templated_question_problem method twice (identical IDs), then delete that method entirely; counts for the other three methods and Mongo corpus_lexicon unchanged. |

Consistent with checklist invariant lines 62-63 (curated behavior belongs in versioned data, not Python conditionals). | Config artifact with model-keyed instruction + version; probe showing two different embedding model ids resolve different instructions and the version lands in diagnostics. |

Failure to touch the P1.7 resolver cache too would recreate exactly the stale-cache class S0's epoch work exists to kill. | Cache test: same query embeds twice with different instruction_version -> two cache entries, zero cross-hits; resolver cache probe showing a version flip forces a miss. |

The 0.98-cosine promotion-gate precedent (checklist:815) is the house pattern for this kind of compatibility number — preregister the analogous bound. | A/B recall diff >= preregistered margin + compatibility probe artifact: document collections unmodified and instructed-vs-uninstructed score-distribution shift within the preset bound. |

| P2X:L1089 | Connect trustworthy concept provenance to final document-anchor protection. | work (open) | build | Wire trustworthy concept provenance into final document-anchor protection THROUGH the single reservation discipline: route it via reservation_policy (P0.3 calibrated gate) so a provenance-backed document can hold a protected seat — do NOT add a fourth ad-hoc shield beside protected lanes, corpus reservation seats, and evidence_allocation protect_fn. | backend service (retriever ranking/reservation) | backend/services/retriever/reservation_policy.py, backend/services/retriever/ranking_policy.py (_is_confident_document_anchor:91-95), backend/services/retriever/document_anchor.py | S11 | Provenance-trusted documents dropped at the final trim are the last-mile failure that turns correct concept routing into a wrong answer. | risky — Registry 'protected lane' row warns explicitly: P2.8's 'document-anchor protection' would be a FOURTH protect mechanism — 'wire it through reservation_policy's single-discipline contract, not a new ad-hoc shield.' TERMINOLOGY: 'anchor' here is sense (2) DocumentAnchorRetriever/document_anchor (title-recall lane + confidence gates), NOT P1.3 conversation anchoring (registry 'anchoring' row pins P2.8/checklist:1110 to sense 2). 'Trustworthy' must be defined via the L1091 gates before this can be enforced — sequencing dependency inside P2.8. | Asserting test: a document nominated by validated concept provenance survives final-packet trimming via a reservation_policy seat (metadata shows the reservation source); no new standalone protection code path introduced (code-review check). |
| P2X:L1091 | Require profile presence and validated source support. | work (open) | build | Define and enforce the trust gate for L1088/L1089: a concept may route/protect documents only when (a) its lexicon entry has the required profile fields present and (b) source support is validated (e.g. source_document_support with count>=threshold across >=2 distinct documents — the >=3/>=2 pattern card_builder.py:134 already uses). Blocked in full until P2.1/S5 lands semantic_profile — gate on the fields that exist now (source_document_support, utility_gloss, provenance methods) and extend when semantic_profile ships. | backend service (vocabulary trust gate) | backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py (admission/trust gates), backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py (profile fields), later services touched by P2.1 semantic_profile | S11 | Without a presence+support gate, thin or junk concepts would reserve documents and the anchor protection (L1089) would amplify noise instead of signal. | risky — TERMINOLOGY AND DEPENDENCY: 'profile presence' most plausibly means the P2.1 lexicon semantic_profile — which is PLAN-ONLY (registry_latent: 'semantic_profile (to-be-built P2.1 lexicon/card field)', grep = zero code hits). Requiring presence of a field that exists nowhere would gate EVERYTHING off. Feasible now only in the reduced form (existing profile-ish fields + validated source_document_support); the full form waits on S5/P2.1. State which form is being closed. | Trust-gate code + test: concept lacking profile fields or validated support contributes zero document nominations/protections; diagnostics count gated-out concepts; receipt names the exact field set enforced (v1: existing fields; v2: + semantic_profile post-S5). |
| P2X:L1092 | Detect polluted concept cards that would mis-reserve documents. | work (open) | build | Build a polluted-card detector (no code exists — grep 'polluted' in backend = zero hits): flag librarian cards/lexicon entries whose document support is dominated by the DF-worthless broad facet family (agency_preservation on 89,088 parents, emotional_patterns on 38,492 — 71-100% of every corpus, V) or by hub/junk concepts, and block flagged cards from document reservation until reviewed; align thresholds with the S7 owner decision on that facet family. | backend service (card/lexicon admission) + junior-agent (detector script) | backend/services/librarian/card_builder.py, backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py, mongo librarian_cards + corpus_lexicon; S7 decision record | S11 | A polluted card mis-reserves documents at the exact point L1089 makes reservations powerful — detection must land with or before the protection. | feasible — Green-field but well-scoped: the contamination signal is already quantified (PLAN_CRITIQUE section 2: broad-alias family stamps 71-100%, 'DF-worthless as a discriminator'; Audit Delta 2 confirms agentic_ai stamped on a film corpus). Interacts with S7 (owner DF-facet decision) and S8 (pair card rebuild) — detector should run as part of the S8 rebuild acceptance so freshly rebuilt PoC-pair cards ship clean. TERMINOLOGY: 'concept cards' = librarian_card.v0 (registry_librarian canonical), not lexicon entries — but pollution originates in lexicon/facet stamps, so detect at both layers. | Detector run over PoC-pair cards producing a flagged list with reasons (facet-family dominance / hub degree); asserting test that a flagged card cannot grant a document reservation; counts reported before/after the S8 rebuild. |

### P3 Thematic RAPTOR + Storage Experiments (frozen lines 1094-1184, chunk P3A)

| ID | Claim (verbatim) | Kind / status | Disposition | Auditable action | Who | Where | When | Why | Feasibility | Required receipt |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P3A:L1098 | Start with `markbuildsbrands_transcripts` or another small healthy corpus. | work (open) | build | At S13 kickoff, pin the pilot corpus in the P3.1 experiment manifest: probe markbuildsbrands_transcripts health (parents=1,015, docs=103, 100% summary attribution per PLAN_CRITIQUE section 2) and record corpus_id + collection names resolved via qdrant_writer._col_for_corpus. | junior-agent (health probe + manifest); plan of record already names mark | docs/PLAN_CRITIQUE_2026-07-13.md S13 row; backend/services/storage/qdrant_writer.py:167 (_col_for_corpus); Mongo parent_chunks/documents for corpus markbuildsbrands_transcripts; new experiment manifest under docs/ or CONTINUITY/ | S13 | a small verified-healthy corpus keeps the thematic pilot cheap and its quality signal interpretable | feasible — Plan of record S13 already resolves the 'or another' clause to mark ('P3.1 thematic pilot (mark)'); PLAN_CRITIQUE section 2 verifies mark at 1,015 parents / 100% heading_path / 100% summary attribution. Caveat: mark has ZERO latent_concepts until S4's paid capture pass — running the pilot before S4/S8 would cluster over pre-capture artifacts (critique C1/C2 pattern). Terminology: 'theme' has NO registry row and no code referent in this sense; backend already uses the word for two unrelated things (services/discourse.py:229,318 discourse themes; chat_orchestrator.py:234 broad-query regex) — register a canonical artifact name (e.g. theme.v0) before build. | Experiment manifest artifact naming corpus_id + a probe transcript (parents=1,015, attribution 100%) with exit-0 assertion, dated at S13 start. |
| P3A:L1099 | Snapshot/scroll existing parent vectors without re-embedding. | work (open) | build | Write a read-only scroll-snapshot script over the pilot corpus's parent summary points (chunk_type='summary', with_vectors=True) into a versioned snapshot file; assert vector count == parent count and zero calls to the embedder sidecar during the run. | junior-agent (script, pattern already exists in scripts_migrate_multitenant.py scroll loop) | backend/services/storage/qdrant_writer.py (corpus_<id8>_naive/hrag, summary points keyed parent_id, :888); new backend/scripts snapshot script; snapshot artifact on disk | S13 | clustering must reuse already-paid embeddings so the pilot's storage/update-cost numbers are honest | feasible — Qdrant scroll with_payload+with_vectors is proven in-repo (scripts_migrate_multitenant.py:73-83). Terminology: 'parent vectors' = Qdrant summary points (parent-level, chunk_type='summary') per registry_leftover 'summary points' — NOT the Tier-0 doc_summary vectors in polymath_doc_summaries (registry_hierarchy 'doc summary' collision); the script must filter chunk_type explicitly or it will mix levels. | Snapshot file with N==1,015 vectors + embedder sidecar request counter unchanged before/after (asserting wrapper, non-zero exit on mismatch). |
| P3A:L1100 | Compare clustering methods and stability across corpus epochs, including soft/non-exclusive membership rather than forcing each parent into one exclusive thema… | work (open) | build | Build a clustering comparison harness over the vector snapshot: HDBSCAN (already in-repo pattern) vs k-means vs a soft-membership method (GMM posteriors or HDBSCAN membership_vector), scored on stability (ARI/AMI across two corpus epochs) and membership-entropy; emit per-method report. | junior-agent (offline analysis script; no service changes) | reuse pattern from backend/services/graph/analytics.py:356-375 (sklearn HDBSCAN + k-means fallback); new offline script + report artifact; input = S13 snapshot | S13 | soft multi-membership is the core hypothesis separating a thematic layer from the existing single-path tree | feasible — sklearn 1.3 HDBSCAN is already imported in backend (graph/analytics.py:375) so no new dependency class. 'Corpus epochs' should reuse the P1.7 epoch notion (lexicon/cache epoch invalidation, S0) rather than inventing a second epoch counter — registry_alias/corpus_lexicon family owns 'epoch'. Risk: 1,015 parents is small for stability statistics; report must state confidence limits. | Comparison report artifact with per-method stability scores across >=2 epochs and a soft-membership distribution; committed with the chosen method + parameters recorded. |
| P3A:L1103 | Allow one parent, section, document, and concept to belong to multiple evidence-backed themes. | work (open) | build | Specify the theme-membership schema so one parent/section/document/concept can hold multiple memberships, each with evidence: member_kind + member_id + strength + evidence_ids; pin the ID space per kind before implementation. | backend ingestion/librarian (schema); owner reviews the ID-space pinning | new theme artifact schema (Mongo collection + optional Qdrant projection); ID sources: parent_chunks.parent_id, summary_tree node ids, documents.doc_id, corpus_lexicon lexicon_id | S13 | non-exclusive membership is what lets broad questions recruit evidence from several themes at once | feasible — TERMINOLOGY (multiple registry hits): 'concept' here MUST bind to corpus_lexicon entries (lexicon_id) — registry_latent 'concept contract' orders a split between latent_concepts (parent capture), summary_tree.concepts (tree_concepts), and the lexicon contract; theme membership over raw strings would recreate the aliases-read-by-nobody dead end (critique C3). 'Section' must be pinned to summary_tree node ids, remembering registry_hierarchy's warning that TreeNode.parent_ids are MEMBER parent-chunk ids, not tree ancestry. | Schema doc + validator (P0.8 style) showing a worked example of one parent and one lexicon_id belonging to two themes with evidence_ids; validator test exit 0. |
| P3A:L1105 | Allow one query and each of its sub-queries to activate multiple themes within and across selected corpora. | work (open) | build | Implement query-time theme activation returning a SET of themes per query and per sub-query, filtered to the user-selected corpus boundary; assert the original-query lane is untouched by theme expansion. | backend retriever (activation stage) + junior-agent tests | retrieval planning seam (backend/services/retriever/, alongside planned-pool fusion); theme index from L1109; selected_corpus_ids filter | S13 | multi-theme activation is the router behavior that should lift broad/cross-document recall | feasible — Two standing invariants bind this: cross-corpus tests preserve the exact user-selected corpus boundary, and the original query remains a protected retrieval lane (checklist Non-Negotiable Invariants). registry_leftover 'corpus boundary' canonical = selected_corpus_ids filter. Sub-queries should reuse the existing decomposer lane rather than a second decomposition path. | Three-tier probe transcript showing >=2 activated themes on a broad query, zero out-of-boundary corpus_ids in candidates, and an unchanged original-query lane (diagnostics assertion, non-zero exit on violation). |
| P3A:L1107 | Generate evidence-backed summaries for themes with member parent IDs, membership strength, and source provenance. | work (open) | build | Generate theme summaries as a new provenance-stamped artifact class: each row carries member parent IDs, membership strength, source provenance, plus summary_model/engine/schema_version/validation status per the P0.1 discipline; no empty-model placeholders. | backend ingestion (Ghost A-style generation path) + junior-agent verify script | new theme summary store (Mongo); generation via the existing summary provider stack; validators in backend/services/storage/schema_validators.py | S13 | evidence-backed theme summaries are what make themes usable for routing without hallucinated abstraction | feasible — This creates a PAID artifact class — standing rule 1 (capture-before-rebuild) applies: before generating, list open capture hooks for the summary seam; at S13 the S1/S4 temporal+latent capture should already be landed, so the seam is clean. Invariant: 'Summary...artifacts carry explicit model/engine, schema version, evidence, and validation status' is verbatim in the ledger. Theme count is small (tens), so cost is trivial vs S4. | Verify script (P0.1-style) over the theme summary store: 0 empty-model rows, 100% rows with member parent IDs + strengths + evidence provenance; exit 0. |
| P3A:L1109 | Index themes with explicit abstraction level, version, corpus ownership, and soft member bindings. | work (open) | build | Design theme index storage (abstraction level, version, corpus ownership, soft member bindings) AGAINST the P3.3 target collection layout, not the legacy per-corpus layout; if a Qdrant projection is added, corpus_id rides the payload with a keyword index and the theme's first consumer (the router, L1113) ships in the same phase. | backend storage + retriever; owner ratifies the storage placement | new Mongo theme collection + optional Qdrant projection; layout decision coupled to P3.3 (qdrant_writer._col_for_corpus is today's only legal resolver per registry_leftover 'qdrant collections') | S13 | a versioned, corpus-owned theme index is what makes themes re-buildable and auditable per epoch | feasible — Standing rule 2 (no dark fields) binds: the theme index must ship with its named consumer or be marked dark data. Standing rule 4 (one migration per seam): P3.1 storage and P3.3 consolidation both touch Qdrant collection layout in the same S13 window — co-schedule so the theme index is not created in a layout P3.3 immediately migrates away from. | Schema + storage-decision note referencing the P3.3 design; probe showing themes queryable by (corpus_id, version, abstraction level) with soft bindings present. |
| P3A:L1111 | Fuse evidence across activated themes; never use a single thematic winner as a hard gate over all downstream retrieval. | work (open) | build | Implement theme-evidence fusion as an additional candidate lane merged by the existing planned-pool fusion, with an explicit test that a single top theme CANNOT zero out other lanes (no hard gate); themes contribute candidates and priors only. | backend retriever | backend/services/retriever/ planned-pool fusion seam (registry_retrieval canonical: planned_pool_fusion / fuse_planned_pools); diagnostics | S13 | fusion-not-gating preserves recall for queries whose answer spans or defies the theme partition | feasible — Registry_retrieval 'fusion' is DISTINCT-verdict: four fusion mechanisms exist (planned_pool_fusion, qdrant_rrf, rank_fusion_fallback, multi_score_fusion) — this claim means planned_pool_fusion; naming it loosely risks wiring themes into the wrong layer. Also aligned with the production-redesign direction (cross-encoder = sole scoring authority): themes route, the CE scores. | Unit/e2e test: with theme A dominant, evidence from non-A lanes still reaches the final packet on a crafted query; assertion test exit 0. |

Consistent with the owner-architecture rule that assembly is deterministic and hydration is budget-ruled. | Test asserting a theme summary id never appears in the final evidence packet's source chunks while its member parents (hydrated) do; exit 0. |

| P3A:L1117 | Evaluate broad recall, precision, storage, update cost, and latency. | work (open) | build | Run the pilot evaluation on the P1.1 held-out suite: broad recall, precision, storage bytes for the theme layer, update cost per epoch (wall + paid tokens), and p50/p95 latency delta vs the S8 baseline; publish a before/after artifact. | junior-agent (eval harness run); backend provides diagnostics | heldout suite + scorer (registry_eval canonical 'heldout eval', run_heldout_eval.score, scorer_version-stamped); baselines under docs/baselines; theme store for storage numbers | S13 | the pilot is an Experiment-tagged item — no adoption without before/after numbers by its own status legend | feasible — Blocked-in-order, not blocked-in-fact: the S8 card-rebuild A/B baseline (held-out 56 vs 2026-07-13 baseline) must exist first, which the S-order guarantees. Hypothesis-promotion rule (standing feedback): numeric targets must be set IN ADVANCE of this run, which is what L1122's 'materially' needs (see that row). | Eval artifact (scorer_version-stamped) with all five metric families vs the S8 baseline, plus the pre-registered target sheet it is scored against. |

Depends on L1111's no-hard-gate fusion being real. | Acceptance test run: named heldout query ids each showing >=2 themes with nonzero final-packet contribution; exit 0. |
| P3A:L1122 | Themes materially improve broad/cross-document questions. | acceptance (open) | owner-decision | OWNER: set the numeric 'materially improves' threshold in advance (e.g. +X points broad-recall / cross-document coverage on the heldout broad-question subset at <=Y ms p95 cost), then junior-agent scores the pilot against it. | owner (threshold decision); junior-agent (scored run) | pre-registered target sheet alongside docs/baselines; heldout broad/cross-document query subset | S13 | an un-numbered 'materially' invites post-hoc rationalization of a marginal pilot | needs-owner-decision — The hypothesis-promotion standing feedback is explicit: promote only against numeric targets set in advance. The eval machinery (P1.1 suite, scorer_version) exists to score it; only the number is missing, and only the owner can set it because it trades latency/storage against recall. | Signed pre-registered threshold in the target sheet BEFORE the eval run, then the eval artifact meeting it (or the pilot is recorded as not adopted). |

A grep-driven implementer could 'extend RAPTOR' in the wrong place. | P3A:L1128 | Select only evidence-strong entities/relations spanning multiple corpora. | work (open) | build | Build a Neo4j selection query for entities/relations whose evidence spans >=2 corpora (Entity.corpus_ids[] cardinality >=2) with per-side typed-evidence minimums; emit a candidate list with support counts per corpus. | junior-agent (graph query + report); backend graph service for productionizing | Neo4j typed edges + Entity.corpus_ids[] (corpus isolation = properties + corpus_ids[], never ID-prefixing); ghost_b_extractions.relations as durable evidence source | S13 | bridges are only trustworthy when both corpus sides independently evidence the same entity/relation | feasible — Substrate exists: registry_extraction 'relations' maps the full chain (RelationItem -> ghost_b_extractions.relations -> Neo4j edge -> lexicon factual_relations). Gotchas from memory apply: canonical entity_id uses HYPHENS (neo4j_writer.entity_id_from_name) and indexed-property matching (never function-wrap in WHERE) or the selection query will silently miss or time out. Sequencing: richer after S11's pair re-extraction; S13 order already ensures that. | Candidate-list artifact: N entities/relations each with >=2 corpus_ids and per-side evidence counts; spot-checked sample with real evidence_ids resolving to chunks. |
| P3A:L1129 | Exclude generic/high-degree `RELATES_TO` hubs without typed support. | work (open) | build | Add a hub-exclusion filter to the selection: drop entities whose cross-corpus connectivity is carried only by RELATES_TO (untyped family) or whose graph degree exceeds a percentile threshold without at least one typed relation per side. | junior-agent (filter + measurement) | Neo4j degree query over the RELATION_FAMILY_MAP typed-vs-RELATES_TO split; selection pipeline from L1128 | S13 | high-degree generic hubs create false bridges that route users to unrelated corpora | feasible — Well-grounded: the Ghost-B predicate-floor memory establishes ~50% related_to as the floor for clean entities — meaning RELATES_TO mass is expected and MUST be discounted, not treated as signal. Typed support is queryable because the family map already types edges (registry_extraction). | Before/after filter report: hub count removed, degree distribution, and zero surviving bridges whose only cross-corpus support is RELATES_TO. |
| P3A:L1130 | Store bridge concepts, source corpora, documents, predicates, confidence, and evidence IDs. | work (open) | build | Define and validate the bridge-card row schema {bridge concept id, source corpora, doc_ids, predicates, confidence, evidence_ids} as a new versioned Mongo artifact, with a P0.8-style validator; the evidence predicate must be computed by the SAME module as shelf_engine's bridge role. | backend librarian/graph (schema + writer); junior-agent validator | new Mongo collection (bridge cards); shared predicate extracted from backend/services/librarian/shelf_engine.py (_role_bridge / principle_bridge criteria); schema_validators.py | S13 | a materialized, evidence-carrying bridge row is what makes cross-corpus routing auditable | feasible — TERMINOLOGY + duplicate-implementation risk (registry_librarian 'bridge' row, FAILURE MODE b): P3.2 bridge cards and transfer_edge both materialize the SAME join _role_bridge computes at query time (shared principle/mechanism + different central subjects + two-sided evidence). Build ONE bridge-evidence predicate module consumed by shelf_engine, bridge cards, and transfer_edge, or thresholds will drift into three definitions of 'bridge'. Also note 'bridge' separately names the answerability-gate LLM-bridging behavior — unrelated mechanism, same word. | Schema validator test exit 0 + a sample bridge card whose evidence_ids hydrate to real chunks on BOTH corpora; grep proof that shelf_engine and the card writer import the same predicate module. |
| P3A:L1132 | Use bridge cards for shelf/document routing, not unsupported final claims. | work (open) | build | Wire bridge cards into shelf/document routing only (P1.4 shelf routing seam): cards may nominate corpora/documents as candidates but never appear as final-claim evidence; enforce with the same router-not-evidence pattern as Tier-0 and L1113. | backend retriever/librarian | P1.4 shelf routing seam + shelf_engine document seats; final evidence packet assembly (must exclude card content) | S13 | keeps cross-corpus suggestions as navigation, never as unsupported synthesized claims | feasible — Standing invariant match: 'only deterministic evidence-backed policy may grant final document seats' — bridge cards are deterministic and evidence-backed, so they legally feed seat nomination. Dependency: P1.4 shelf routing is 0/8 boxes today (PLAN_CRITIQUE table) and lands S9-ish via S8/S9 work; by S13 the routing seam exists. Standing rule 2: the card's first consumer (this router) ships in the same phase as the card. | e2e probe: a cross-corpus query shows bridge-card-nominated documents in routing diagnostics while zero bridge-card text appears in final evidence; asserting probe exit 0. |
| P3A:L1133 | Evaluate non-obvious cross-domain recall and false-bridge rate. | work (open) | build | Extend the heldout suite with cross-domain rows (non-obvious two-corpus questions) plus labeled negative pairs; measure cross-domain recall and false-bridge rate (bridges asserted where the negative label says none exists) with pre-registered thresholds. | junior-agent (suite extension + scored run); owner sets thresholds | heldout suite (P1.1) + scorer; bridge-card store for assertions | S13 | false bridges are the failure mode that turns a knowledge tool into a confabulation engine | feasible — The negative-controls machinery exists conceptually in the suite (registry_eval 'negative controls' — keep 'forbidden-vocabulary control' distinct); false-bridge labeling is new manual work on the PoC pair, tractable at pair scale. Numeric thresholds must precede the run (hypothesis-promotion rule). | Eval artifact: cross-domain recall + false-bridge rate vs pre-registered thresholds; negative rows fail closed (0 bridges asserted). |
| P3A:L1134 | Materialize deterministic `transfer_edge` rows when documents with meaningfully different central subjects share a specific validated principle or mechanism. | work (open) | build | Implement the deterministic transfer_edge writer: emit a row only when two documents with different central_subjects (librarian_cards.central_subjects disjointness test) share a validated principle/mechanism (lexicon-ID'd), using the shared bridge-evidence predicate from L1130. | backend librarian/graph (writer); junior-agent tests | new transfer_edge store (Mongo or Neo4j edge class); inputs: librarian_cards.central_subjects, corpus_lexicon entries (principle/mechanism IDs), two-sided evidence_ids | S13 | transfer edges are the durable form of 'this principle transfers across domains' — the product's core promise | feasible — TERMINOLOGY: 'central subjects' is canonical librarian_cards.central_subjects (registry_latent) — and that row's guard applies: central_subjects deliberately EXCLUDES latent_concepts (those live in candidate_latent_subjects and can corroborate but never solely qualify); the disjointness test must run on central_subjects proper. 'Principle/mechanism ID' presumes P2.1's lexicon contract (mechanisms currently live as parent_chunks fields per CONCEPT_SOURCE_FIELDS; the lexicon-ID form arrives via S5/P2.1) — S13 ordering satisfies this, earlier attempts would be blocked. registry_librarian flags transfer_edge as PLAN-ONLY sharing _role_bridge's inputs: reuse the one predicate module. | Writer test: crafted fixture pair produces exactly one transfer_edge with both-side evidence; a same-subject pair produces zero; exit 0. |

| P3A:L1180 | Benchmark current warmed MPS reranker at realistic candidate shapes. | work (open) | build | Benchmark the DEPLOYED reranker (:8081 — verify first WHICH binary serves it: torch fp16 MPS sidecar per current deployment, reranker_mlx is rollback) after S0's warmup merge, at realistic candidate shapes taken from live diagnostics (k x candidate-length distributions from the P1.9 latency-by-shape data), recording embedder co-load state during every run. | junior-agent (benchmark harness) | :8081 sidecar (plist-managed torch fp16 / rollback reranker_mlx); shape distributions from retrieval diagnostics; benchmark artifact under docs/baselines | S13 | an unwarmed or wrong-binary benchmark would misprice the serving alternatives comparison | feasible — TERMINOLOGY (registry_leftover 'reranker readiness' + user memory): THREE implementations answer to 'the reranker' — the deployed torch fp16 MPS sidecar on :8081, the reranker_mlx rollback backend, and a repo /reranker docker service with NO warmup; 'current warmed MPS reranker' must name the :8081 binary explicitly (kickstart-env/plist drift is a known gotcha). 'Warmed' depends on P1.8 warmup code that is IN CODE pending S0's merge — S-order handles it. Metal contention rule (memory): embedder and reranker share ONE Metal GPU; the benchmark must control/record embedder load or p95 is meaningless. | Benchmark artifact: binary identity (endpoint /health or process check) + warm-state proof + throughput/p50/p95 across >=3 realistic shape buckets, embedder-idle and embedder-busy variants. |

Risk is ordinary ML-ops friction (opset/tokenizer mismatches); parity must be scored against the fp16 sidecar's scores, not just rank order, because the calibrated sigma((s-mu)/T) calibration (memory) rides absolute scores. | Export parity artifact (score deltas within stated tolerance on the fixture set) then benchmark table, OR a recorded export-failure note closing the box as skipped-by-guard. |
| P3A:L1182 | Compare throughput, p50/p95, ranking quality, memory, and contention. | work (open) | build | Produce the comparison matrix across serving options: throughput, p50/p95 by shape bucket, ranking quality (rank correlation + heldout nDCG delta vs the deployed sidecar), memory footprint, and Metal/CPU contention (embedder-busy deltas); one table, same fixtures, same hardware. | junior-agent (matrix run) | harness from L1180/L1181; heldout subset for quality; docker/host stats for memory | S13 | the serving decision is multi-axis — a single-axis win (speed) is exactly what L1183 forbids acting on | feasible — All inputs exist by construction of the two prior rows. Quality axis must be measured, not assumed invariant across runtimes: int8 + different kernels can shift calibrated scores enough to reorder marginal candidates, which the answerability gates downstream are sensitive to. Contention axis is mandated by the Metal-GPU memory (never assume the sidecars are isolated). | Single comparison artifact with all five axes for every candidate runtime, fixtures and hardware pinned, dated. |
| P3A:L1183 | Do not switch models or runtimes based on theoretical speed alone. | work (open) | standing-invariant | Keep as the adoption gate: any model/runtime switch requires the L1182 matrix green on quality and contention axes plus an owner sign-off; theoretical or vendor-benchmark speed claims are inadmissible as evidence. | owner-enforced gate | P3.5 adoption decision; deploy path for the :8081 sidecar (plist/LaunchAgent — remember apple-ml sidecars deploy from a COPY, repo edits need re-install) | CONT | the reranker is the sole scoring authority under the production redesign — a mis-switched runtime degrades every answer | feasible — Standing guardrail matching two adopted feedbacks: think-before-executing (predict outcome before model swaps) and hypothesis-promotion (numeric targets in advance). History justifies it: the previous MLX 'cross-encoder' was a bi-encoder cosine sham (memory) — precisely the failure a theoretical-speed switch invites. Deployment gotcha if adopted: sidecars run from a rsynced COPY (~/PolymathRuntime), so a runtime switch needs re-install, not git pull. | Absence of any runtime/model switch commit without a linked L1182 artifact + owner sign-off; if a switch ships, its deploy includes the re-install receipt and a live /health binary check. |

### Quick Upload + Regression Matrix + Strict Ready + Completion Rule (frozen lines 1185-1248, chunk GTE)

| ID | Claim (verbatim) | Kind / status | Disposition | Auditable action | Who | Where | When | Why | Feasibility | Required receipt |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| GTE:L1187 | Quick Upload creates a durable asynchronous batch. | work (x) | done-unverified | Re-verify the durable-batch receipt end-to-end: POST a quick upload against the live backend, assert an ingest_batches row with stored_bytes>0 and item states persists in Mongo, restart the backend container, and assert the batch resumes/remains resumable (self-asserting script, non-zero exit on fail). | junior-agent (asserting e2e probe) | backend/services/ingestion/batches.py (batch creation ~:909-1088); Mongo ingest_batches / ingest_batch_items | LOG | uploads that survive restarts are the precondition for any document ever becoming retrievable evidence | already-done — Ledger [x] with no recorded receipt. What is missing is an e2e restart-survival receipt, which the ledger's own completion contract ('deployed + reconciled + verified') requires. | e2e script exits 0: batch id present in ingest_batches with stored_bytes>0 before AND after `docker compose restart backend`, and items reach a terminal stage afterward. |

Silent-fallback-accounting rule applies: the except-branch walk is a silent fallback with no counter — if the aggregation ever starts failing, quota checks degrade invisibly. Worth a one-line fallback counter, but that is an improvement, not a gap in this claim. | Cited lines batches.py:290-299 (aggregate on stored_bytes) and :298 (walk only in except); optionally a unit test forcing the exception path to prove the fallback still works. |

| GTE:L1200 | Run every row for Fast, Hybrid, and Graph unless the row explicitly tests a tier-specific feature. | standing-rule (na) | standing-invariant | Encode the rule into the matrix runner itself: the S14 harness iterates every query row across RetrievalTier.qdrant_only / qdrant_mongo / qdrant_mongo_graph and refuses to emit a report if any (row, tier) cell is missing without an explicit tier-specific-feature exemption flag on the row spec. | junior-agent (matrix runner) on the three_tier_eval/run_heldout_eval substrate | backend/services/retriever/three_tier_eval.py (ROUTES :25-35), backend/scripts/run_heldout_eval.py, new matrix row spec file | S14 | tier-conditional bugs (e.g. graph-only regressions) are invisible unless every row runs on every tier | feasible — Terminology: 'Fast, Hybrid, Graph' are the informal names; canonical enum is RetrievalTier {qdrant_only, qdrant_mongo, qdrant_mongo_graph} (registry_retrieval 'tier' row — the bare word 'tier' collides with source_tier, Tier-0 routing, and tier_chunker Tiers A/B/C, so the runner must use the enum). This duplicates the first Non-Negotiable Invariant in matrix-scoped form; harness (three_tier_eval ROUTES) already enumerates all three. | Matrix report artifact whose schema is (row x 3 tiers) complete, with any skipped cell carrying an explicit tier_specific_exemption reason; runner exits non-zero on silent gaps. |
