The pass threshold must be set with that reality, and the row must record whether ingest embedding was active during each query. | Cell records with concurrent-ingest flag=true, correct answers, latency within the preregistered contention budget, pressure-gate state captured in the run record. |
| GTE:L1220 | For every run record: | standing-rule (na) | standing-invariant | Encode the record contract as a typed run-record schema the matrix runner emits for EVERY (row, tier) cell; the runner fails the run if any of the eight field groups (L1222-L1229) is missing rather than silently omitting it. | junior-agent (runner schema) + backend diagnostics fields | matrix runner artifact schema (extend EVAL_*.json shape from run_heldout_eval.py); backend chat diagnostics (_schemas_legacy.py diagnostics fields) | S14 | an unrecorded run cannot be audited, compared, or regressed against — the record IS the deliverable | feasible — Standing rule of the matrix, not a one-time box. The EVAL_*.json artifact convention and chat diagnostics (requested_tier/effective_tier etc.) give the skeleton; the eight groups below each have named substrate gaps critiqued per-row. Schema-validate the record (the repo's own Audit Delta 2 found zero Mongo validators — do not repeat that pattern in eval artifacts). | JSON-schema for the run record checked into backend/evals/, runner exits non-zero when a cell record fails validation. |
| GTE:L1222 | Plan, probes, obligations, and original-query lane. | acceptance (open) | build | Add to the run record: the retrieval plan, executed probes, coverage obligations, and explicit confirmation that the original-query protected lane ran unmodified (lane role 'original' present in fused pools). | backend retriever diagnostics + matrix runner | backend/services/retriever/planned_fusion.py (lane roles), chat orchestrator plan/probe diagnostics; run-record schema | S14 | proves per-run that expansions never replaced the user's actual question | feasible — Canonical terms (registry_retrieval): 'original-query lane' = original-query protected lane (lane role 'original' in planned fusion) — already implemented as lane protection; 'obligations' ties to the S5 obligation-aware selection fix for the positional 6-match planner cap (C3), which must exist by S14. Mostly plumbing existing diagnostics into the record. | Sample cell record containing plan, probes[], obligations[], and lanes[] with role='original' marked protected+unmodified. |
| GTE:L1223 | Corpus/document/tree routes and route scores. | acceptance (open) | build | Add to the run record: corpus routes, document routes (Tier-0 routing card hits), tree-descent routes, and the score attached to each route decision. | backend retriever diagnostics + matrix runner | Tier-0 routing (polymath_doc_summaries projection), tree descent (document_gated_adaptive_tree_descent), route diagnostics; run-record schema | S14 | route transparency is what makes a wrong answer debuggable to a wrong turn instead of a mystery | feasible — Terminology (registry_retrieval/hierarchy): 'tree' here = summary_tree descent, 'routes' at document level = tier0_routing_card layer — NOT RetrievalTier despite the shared word; the record schema must use distinct keys (retrieval_tier vs routes[]). Tier-0 is ON (librarian plan seam verified live), so route data exists to capture. | Sample cell record with routes[] = {level: corpus\|document\|tree, target_id, score} for each hop. |
| GTE:L1224 | Vocabulary matches, concept IDs, provenance, and planner expansions. | acceptance (open) | build | Add to the run record: vocabulary matches with their lexicon identity ids, provenance (which trusted alias method matched), and every planner expansion with its source. Define 'concept ID' in the schema as the corpus_lexicon identity id. | backend retriever vocabulary diagnostics + matrix runner | backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py (_TRUSTED_EXACT_ALIAS_METHODS), corpus_lexicon ids, grounded planner call logs; run-record schema | S14 | expansion provenance is the audit trail that keeps the vocabulary bridge honest | risky — TERM COLLISION (registry_alias/registry_latent): 'concept IDs' is ambiguous across four artifacts — corpus_lexicon identities, parent latent_concepts, summary-tree `concepts` (populated on 0/42,990 v2 and 0/1,867 ecom nodes per Audit Delta 2 — recording these would record nothing), and the unbuilt P2.1 concept contract. Schema must pin ONE meaning (lexicon identity id) and name the others separately. Also: 'planner expansions' require the grounded planner to be operational — baseline says model unset → not operational; P1.2 activation is gated behind card.v0+seats+baseline. Until then this field records an honest empty, not a fake. | Sample cell record with vocabulary_matches[] = {lexicon_id, surface, method(provenance)}, planner_expansions[] = {text, source, obligation}; empty-with-reason when planner is off. |
| GTE:L1225 | Candidate counts before/after fusion, reranking, and final selection. | acceptance (open) | build | Add to the run record: candidate counts at each funnel stage — per-lane pre-fusion, post-fusion (named fusion mechanism), post-rerank, and final selection. | backend retriever diagnostics + matrix runner | planned_fusion.py (fuse_planned_pools), reranker service, final selection; run-record schema | S14 | funnel counts localize recall loss to the exact stage that dropped the evidence | feasible — Terminology (registry_retrieval, verdict DISTINCT): 'fusion' names several mechanisms — planned_pool_fusion (lane merge), qdrant_rrf, rank_fusion_fallback (the reranker-timeout escape), multi_score_fusion (slated for retirement under the production redesign). The record must name WHICH fusion ran; a rank_fusion_fallback run is a degraded run and must be visible as such (silent-fallback accounting rule). | Sample cell record with candidate_counts = {per_lane:{...}, post_fusion, fusion_kind, post_rerank, final} and rerank_fallback flag when the breaker fired. |
| GTE:L1226 | Corpus/document distribution and reservation reasons. | acceptance (open) | build | Add to the run record: final-set corpus/document distribution and, for every reserved seat, the reservation reason emitted by the calibrated reservation policy. | backend retriever diagnostics + matrix runner | backend/services/retriever/reservation_policy.py, ranking_policy.py; run-record schema | S14 | reservation reasons are the receipts that seats were earned by policy, not by floor quota | feasible — Best-substrated field group in the contract: reservation_policy.py (P0.3) is merged and live-probed (PLAN_CRITIQUE section 2). Terminology (registry_leftover 'document seats'): qualify seat layer — evidence seat (reservation/ranking_policy) vs document seat (librarian shelf_engine); this field group records the former. | Sample cell record with distribution = {by_corpus, by_doc} and reservations[] = {chunk_id, reason, policy_version}. |
| GTE:L1227 | Answerability, lane coverage, evidence coverage, and refusal reason. | acceptance (open) | build | Add to the run record: the answerability verdict, recall-lane coverage AND evidence-lane coverage as two separate fields, evidence coverage, and the refusal reason when refusing. | backend chat/answerability diagnostics + matrix runner | services/answerability_tuning.py knobs, chat gate (_build_retrieval_answerability_gate), retriever sufficiency (_evaluate_sufficiency); run-record schema | S14 | separating 'could not find' from 'chose not to answer' is what makes refusals tunable | feasible — TERM COLLISION flagged by registry_retrieval: 'lane coverage' has TWO real senses — recall_lane_coverage (selection.lane_coverage) and evidence_lane_coverage; the claim's wording lists 'lane coverage, evidence coverage' which maps cleanly IF the schema keeps both names distinct. Answerability substrate is mature (P0.4 7 [x]; twin gates documented: retriever sufficiency = repair driver kept STRICT, chat gate = refusal arbiter). | Sample cell record with {answerability_verdict, recall_lane_coverage, evidence_lane_coverage, evidence_coverage, refusal_reason\|null}. |
| GTE:L1228 | Embed, vocabulary, tree, retrieval, graph, rerank, hydrate, and total time. | acceptance (open) | build | Add to the run record: per-stage wall times — embed, vocabulary, tree, retrieval, graph, rerank, hydrate — plus total, sourced from in-request stage timers (not log scraping). | backend per-stage timing instrumentation + matrix runner | chat orchestrator / retriever stage timers; overlaps P1.9 'per-stage timing' open item; probe_tier_latency.py precedent; run-record schema | S14 | stage timings turn 'it was slow' into 'rerank ate 28s', enabling targeted fixes | feasible — Per-stage timing is itself an open P1.9 box (17 open) — this record field and that box should land as one change, not two. The three-tier latency probe already measures totals (Fast 39.8s degraded / Hybrid 14.0s / Graph 18.9s baseline), so the delta is stage-level attribution inside the request. | Sample cell record with timings_ms = {embed, vocabulary, tree, retrieval, graph, rerank, hydrate, total} summing sanely against measured wall time. |
| GTE:L1229 | Selected source IDs, calibrated scores, and evidence text. | acceptance (open) | build | Add to the run record: final selected source IDs (corpus_id/doc_id/parent/child identity), calibrated reranker scores, and the evidence text actually cited. | backend final-selection diagnostics + matrix runner | final evidence packet fields; reranker calibrated scores (torch_fp16 sidecar, sigma((s-mu)/T)); run-record schema | S14 | the record must let an auditor re-read exactly what the model saw, or 'verified' means nothing | feasible — Substrate exists end-to-end: the final-evidence invariant already requires corpus_id/doc_id/parent-child identity/provenance on evidence, and the :8081 reranker emits calibrated fp16 cross-encoder scores. One caution: run records containing heldout evidence text are eval artifacts — keep them under docs/baselines or backend/evals, never in any persisted retrieval representation, per the eval_firewall contamination rule. | Sample cell record with sources[] = {corpus_id, doc_id, parent_id, child_id, calibrated_score, evidence_text}. |
| GTE:L1233 | A corpus is strict-ready only when: | invariant (na) | build | Implement strict_ready as a computed, per-corpus readiness state (the ten gates L1235-L1247) inside the S6 readiness split — a distinct level ABOVE fully_enriched, published via corpus_readiness — rather than leaving it prose. | backend readiness service | backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py; Mongo corpus_readiness (schema corpus_readiness.v2); S6 readiness-split design | S6 | a machine-checked strict_ready is what stops 'ready' from meaning whatever the last human believed | feasible — Registry_readiness: 'strict ready' is PLAN-ONLY (canonical strict_ready, zero code symbols today) and the plan already routes it: 'Strict-ready definition ... consumed by readiness split (S6)' (PLAN_CRITIQUE section 1). S6 explicitly ends fully_enriched implying librarian-grade metadata; strict_ready becomes the strong tier alongside metadata_quality_readiness and temporal_capability. | corpus_readiness rows expose strict_ready with per-gate booleans + failure reasons; UGO canary flips gates on/off in a test and the rollup follows. |
| GTE:L1235 | Every eligible document is parsed, structured, indexed, and queryable. | acceptance (open) | build | Implement gate 1: every eligible document has ingest_stage in the strict set (parsed, structured, indexed) AND is queryable — using the strict fully_enriched stage set, not the weaker QUERYABLE_INGEST_STAGES. | backend readiness service | backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py (FULLY_ENRICHED_STAGES :112, per-doc stage rollup); documents.ingest_stage | S6 | one unparsed document silently missing from retrieval is exactly the failure strict_ready exists to catch | feasible — TERM TRAP flagged by registry_readiness: QUERYABLE_INGEST_STAGES is deliberately WEAKER than fully_enriched (includes pending-graph/summary variants, ingestion_service.py:39-47) — a strict_ready gate written against 'queryable' would pass documents that fail the claim's own 'parsed, structured, indexed' clause. Gate must be stage==fully_enriched AND queryable, with 'eligible' defined by the L1245 denominator rule. | Gate function + test: corpus with one document at a pending stage reports strict_ready=false naming that doc_id. |
| GTE:L1236 | Qdrant child-vector counts match durable Mongo child counts. | acceptance (open) | build | Implement gate 2: per-corpus Qdrant child-vector point count equals durable Mongo child-chunk count (per collection, exact), surfaced with both numbers and the delta. | backend readiness service + reconciliation script | per-corpus Qdrant collections (_col_for_corpus) vs Mongo chunks; readiness.py index-projection gates (:368-454 precedent) | S6 | a Mongo-Qdrant count gap means some children are unsearchable — invisible recall loss | feasible — Cheap deterministic count compare; readiness.py already min-gates fully_enriched on index-projection readiness (lexicon_ready / summary_tree_index_ready / document_profile_index_ready), so this is one more min-gate in an existing pattern. Historical lesson (vector=0 while health stayed green) is precisely why this must be a count parity check, not a health-endpoint check. | Gate emits {mongo_children, qdrant_points, delta:0} per corpus; test with one deleted point flips strict_ready=false. |
| GTE:L1237 | Every summary-required parent has one validated, provider-attributed, child-evidenced summary. | acceptance (open) | build | Implement gate 3: every summary-required parent has exactly one summary that is (a) schema-validated, (b) provider-attributed (summary_model non-empty), and (c) child-evidenced (child IDs/boundaries/evidence fields verified) — reusing p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify as the gate body. | backend readiness service, reusing the P0.1 verify harness | backend p0_1_summary_integrity.py; parent_chunks summary fields; summary_jobs | S6 | unattributed or unevidenced summaries poison every downstream tree route and card | feasible — Strongest live receipt in this section: P0.1 verify PASSES all four corpora today (V — 0 empty-model, 86,880+9,453+1,009+203 all attributed; the checklist's two IN PROGRESS tags are stale per standing rule 3). Gap: the 'child-evidenced' clause maps to P0.1's still-open verify boxes (child IDs/boundaries/evidence field verification) — the gate is only as strong as that verify extension, so land it with this gate. | strict_ready gate wraps `p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify` per corpus, exit 0 required, including the extended child-evidence field checks. |
| GTE:L1239 | Every document summary tree is synchronized. | acceptance (open) | build | Implement gate 4: per-document summary_tree synchronization check — every document's tree nodes reconcile with current parents (no orphan sections, no missing rollups, passthrough links intact) and the tree's Qdrant index projection matches Mongo. | backend readiness service + summary-tree reconcile | Mongo summary_tree (21,432 sections / 23,394 rollups baseline); summary_tree_index_ready gate (readiness.py); passthrough fields (registry_hierarchy) | S6 | a desynchronized tree routes descent into sections that no longer exist | feasible — Terminology (registry_hierarchy): 'summary tree' = canonical summary_tree (the RAPTOR tree — same artifact). 'Synchronized' needs a checkable definition; the substrate exists (summary_tree_index_ready already min-gates fully_enriched; registry_readiness lists a distinct 'summary-job reconcile' and 'tier0 projection reconcile' — this gate composes them per document rather than inventing a new mechanism). | Gate emits per-doc tree_sync=true with counts (sections, rollups, passthroughs, orphans:0); test that deletes one tree node flips the gate. |
| GTE:L1240 | Vocabulary projection is complete for the selected extraction contract. | acceptance (open) | build | Implement gate 5: corpus_lexicon vocabulary projection is complete FOR the corpus's selected extraction contract — every reconciled identity from the active engine's extractions is present in the lexicon and its Qdrant schema payload projection (corpus_{cid8}_schemas) is current-epoch. | backend readiness service + lexicon build | backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py; Qdrant corpus_{cid8}_schemas payloads; lexicon_ready gate (readiness.py:368-454); extraction engine selection (Mongo settings endpoints[].enabled) | S6 | an incomplete lexicon silently disables the vocabulary bridge for exactly the newest content | feasible — lexicon_ready already min-gates fully_enriched — the delta is 'for the selected extraction contract': registry_extraction warns 'ghost_b' names both the stage and one engine, and engine='local' now means a provider LLM, so the gate must read the corpus's ACTIVE engine/contract (schema_version + extractor on ghost_b_extractions) not assume one. Note Audit Delta 2: `extractor` was absent on 0/3,000 sampled rows — that attribution backfill is a prerequisite for this gate to be computable per-contract. | Gate emits {active_contract, identities_expected, lexicon_entries, projection_epoch_current:true}; S5 epoch bump exercises the invalidation path. |
| GTE:L1241 | Required graph extraction is validated and promoted. | acceptance (open) | build | Implement gate 6: for corpora where graph extraction is required, all durable extractions are schema-validated AND promoted to Neo4j (graph_promotion_jobs complete, zero pending/failed promotions). | backend readiness service + graph promotion jobs | Mongo ghost_b_extractions (+ ghost_b_failures); backend/services/ingestion/graph_promotion_jobs.py; Neo4j | S6 | extractions that never promote are dead weight — paid extraction earning zero retrieval value | feasible — Promotion machinery exists (graph_promotion_jobs.py:264 — 'ghost_b_extractions is the durable extraction artifact store', promotion to Neo4j), so the gate is a job-state + validation query. Scope honestly: 'required' must be per-corpus policy (extraction is being re-run for the pair at S11 per the signed disposition matrix), and the known extraction-to-retrieval-metadata gap means promoted-to-Neo4j is the correct bar here — do not stretch this gate to cover the separate promote()-to-payload program. | Gate emits {extractions, validated, promoted, pending:0, failed:0} per corpus with graph required; corpus with one unpromoted row reports strict_ready=false. |
| GTE:L1242 | No durable failed/running/duplicate job remains. | acceptance (open) | build | Implement gate 7: zero durable jobs in failed or running (stale-lease) state and zero duplicate active jobs for the corpus, across summary_jobs and the other durable job collections. | backend readiness service + job reconciliation | Mongo summary_jobs (baseline: 50,522 succeeded / 500 queued / 775 superseded), job_leases, source_parse_jobs, graph_promotion_jobs | S6 | a lingering failed/duplicate job means some artifact is stale or double-written under the corpus you are about to trust | feasible — Straight Mongo state query; lease semantics exist (P0.6, lane lease vs cleanup lease distinction per registry_leftover). Baseline shows 500 queued summary jobs — under this gate no corpus with queued work is strict_ready, which is correct strictness, not a gate bug: strict_ready SHOULD be false while durable work is in flight, and the caller waits. Define 'running' violations as lease-expired-stale jobs so healthy in-flight leases are reported but not misclassified as wedged. | Gate emits per-collection {failed:0, stale_running:0, duplicates:0, queued:0} for the corpus; seeded failed job flips the gate in a test. |
| GTE:L1243 | No ontology predicate/entity-type violation remains under the active contract. | acceptance (open) | build | Implement gate 8: validate all promoted graph edges/entities for the corpus against the ACTIVE ontology contract (allowed predicates, entity types); zero violations remain. | backend readiness service + ontology validator | Neo4j (promoted graph); ontology contract definition (P2.5 typed signatures territory); validator new | S6 | ontology violations turn the graph tier into a source of confidently-typed nonsense | risky — Weakest-substrate gate in the section: there is no machine-readable ontology contract yet (P2.5 typed signatures 0 of 4, P2.4 negation 0 of 5 — both S11), so 'the active contract' does not exist as an artifact to validate against until S11 defines it. Feasible as a gate SHELL at S6 (contract-absent → gate reports not-applicable-with-reason, never silently true), with real enforcement landing at S11. Known floor: ~50% related_to is the correct pure-BERT floor on clean entities — the contract must encode that reality, not wish it away. | Gate emits {contract_id, violations:0} when a contract is active, or {contract_id:null, status:'no_active_contract'} — never a bare pass; post-S11 run on the pair shows real validation counts. |
| GTE:L1245 | Readiness denominators exclude only explicit, documented duplicates or structural skips. | acceptance (open) | build | Implement the denominator rule: readiness denominators may exclude a document ONLY via an explicit exclusion record (kind: documented-duplicate \| structural-skip, with reason + date + actor); anything else counts against strict_ready. | backend readiness service | backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py denominator computation; documents exclusion fields / dedup records (containment-based doc-dedup pipeline precedent) | S6 | quietly shrinking denominators is how 100% ready gets faked; explicit exclusions keep the percentage honest | feasible — The dedup program already produces exactly the durable duplicate records this rule needs (containment-based detect/prevent/correct pipeline with duplicate endpoints), so 'documented duplicate' has an artifact to point at. This is the anti-gaming clause of the whole section — it should be reviewed as adversarially as code: any new exclusion kind requires a ledger note. | Readiness output lists exclusions[] = {doc_id, kind, reason, recorded_by, date} and denominator math is reproducible from it; test that an undocumented skip fails the gate. |
| GTE:L1247 | All three retrieval tiers pass smoke and negative-control probes. | acceptance (open) | build | Implement gate 10: a scripted per-corpus probe suite that runs one smoke query AND one negative-control query on each of the three RetrievalTier values (6 probes) and requires all to pass (smoke answers grounded; negative controls refuse) before strict_ready flips true. | backend readiness service invoking the eval harness | three_tier_eval.py routes + negative_control probes; wired as the final min-gate in readiness.py; exercised corpus-wide at S14 | S6 | strict_ready must mean 'provably answers and provably refuses' on every tier, not just 'data present' | feasible — All parts exist: three-tier harness enumerates the tiers, negative_control is an established shape, answerability gates fail closed (P0.4). Design decision to make explicit: this gate makes readiness computation run live queries (cost + latency + GPU contention) — run it on readiness RECOMPUTE events, not on every readiness read, and record probe artifacts. S14's full matrix is the corpus-wide superset of this per-corpus gate. | Per-corpus probe artifact: 3 tiers x {smoke: grounded answer, negative_control: refusal with reason} all green, referenced by the strict_ready=true readiness row. |
